Big Room House
Big Room House is mainstage EDM-house (late 2000s→early 2010s Europe) built for
arenas: 126–132 BPM four-on-the-floor, huge riser breakdowns, then minimal,
bass-heavy drops with supersaws and “big hall” reverb. Popularized by festival hits
like Sandro Silva & Quintino’s “Epic” and artists such as Hardwell and Martin Garrix.

Genre Tags:
Big room house, Big room, Mainstage house, Festival house, EDM house,

Mood Tags:
Bombastic, anthemic, euphoric, triumphant, explosive, adrenaline, high-energy,
hands-in-the-air, dramatic, epic, massive, intense, aggressive, relentless, driving,
propulsive, punchy, powerful, celebratory, victorious, ravey, cinematic, tense-build,
cathartic, charged, bold, festival-ready, hype, fiery, electrifying, showdown,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, 909 kick, punchy festival kick, kick-as-bass drop, sub bass
layer, offbeat closed hi-hat, open-hat lifts, clap stack, snare build rolls, tom build
fills, white-noise risers, pitch risers, uplifter FX, downlifter FX, impact hits, reverse
cymbals, big hall reverb, supersaw stack lead, detuned saw stabs, big room pluck
lead, simple stab chords, sidechain pump, gated noise sweeps, mono drop bass,
minimal drop arrangement,

Vocal Tags:
Minimal vocal hook, one-liner drop cue, chant hook, spoken hype lines, sampled
vocal phrase, vocal chops, pitched vocal chops, stuttered vocal edits, female hook
vocal, male hook vocal, processed lead vocal, reverb-delay throws, short topline

Future House
Future House is a 2010s house offshoot built for big, clean drops: 4/4 at 124–128
BPM, bouncy FM bass leads, metallic pluck synths, and EDM-style breakdown
builds. The term was coined by Tchami and the sound was popularized by Oliver
Heldens and Don Diablo; Beatport added it as a genre tag in 2016

Genre Tags:
Future house, Future house EDM, Mainstage future house, Festival future house

Mood Tags:
Bouncy, upbeat, bright, euphoric, uplifting, confident, swaggering, playful, flirty,
feel-good, glossy, shiny, punchy, energetic, propulsive, driving, peak-time,
festival-ready, bold, triumphant, celebratory, cheeky, sassy, funky, groovy, springy,
carefree, radiant, clean, modern, high-impact, addictive,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, 909 kick, punchy layered kick, clap on 2&4, snare build rolls,
offbeat closed hi-hat, open-hat lifts, ride cymbal lift, shaker loop, percussion fills,
sidechain pump, FM bass lead, rubbery bass pluck, pitch-bent bass slides, mono
drop bass, metallic pluck lead, detuned saw stabs, short chord stabs, organ-bass
layer, bright top synth riff, white-noise risers, pitch risers, uplifter FX, impact hits,
reverse cymbals, filter sweeps, vocal chop accents,

Vocal Tags:
Vocal chops, pitched vocal chops, chopped hook loop, sampled vocal phrases,
one-liner drop cue, spoken hype line, female hook vocal, male hook vocal, pop
topline chorus, minimal verse, chant hook, call-and-response hook, doubled hook
lines, harmony-stacked hook, stuttered vocal edits, reverb-delay throws, ad-lib hype
tags, vocal-free drops, a cappella breakdown moment, crowd-chant outro,

Bass House
Bass House is mid-2010s club house that fuses four-on-the-floor (124–130 BPM)
with aggressive, modulated bass drops and UK bass/garage/bassline swing. It broke
big via Jauz’s “Feel the Volume” and scenes like AC Slater’s Night Bass.

Genre Tags:
Bass house, Club bass house, Underground bass house, UK bass house

Mood Tags:
Bouncy, rowdy, hype, aggressive, punchy, gritty, dirty, distorted, menacing, cheeky,
playful, high-energy, adrenaline, urgent, explosive, driving, propulsive, relentless,
sweaty-club, underground, rebellious, bold, brash, chaotic-fun, sinister-funk,
confrontational, ravey, bass-face, energized, edgy, spicy, kinetic, nasty, tough,
cocky, late-night, feral, party-starting,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, punchy layered kick, 909 kick, clap on 2&4, snappy snare,
snare build rolls, tom build fills, offbeat closed hi-hat, open-hat lifts, swung hats,
shuffled percussion, garage swing shuffle, breakbeat fills, sub bass layer,
modulated mid-bass, FM bass lead, rubber bass pluck, LFO wobble bass, growl
bass, reese bass stab, pitch-bent bass slides, heavy distortion, saturation, bitcrush
accents, metallic pluck hits, short chord stabs, rave stab chords, vocal chop

Vocal Tags:
Vocal chops, pitched vocal chops, sampled shout phrases, spoken hype lines,
one-liner drop cue, chant hook, crowd chant outro, rap verse feature, short topline
chorus, minimal verse, ad-lib hype tags, stuttered vocal edits, reverb-delay throws,
vocal-free drops, a cappella breakdown moment, doubled hook lines, harmonized
hook stack, whispered spoken line, filtered vocal snippets, glitchy vocal cuts

Deep House
Deep House is a soulful offshoot of house that emerged mid-1980s, pioneered by
Larry Heard (Mr. Fingers) with tracks like “Mystery of Love” and “Can You Feel It” .
Often 120–125 BPM, it favors warm chords, deep bass, and long, hypnotic grooves

Genre Tags:
Deep house, Classic deep house, Deep soulful house, Deep vocal house

Mood Tags:
Warm, soulful, smooth, lush, velvety, intimate, romantic, sexy, late-night,
afterhours, nocturnal, hypnotic, deep, mellow, chilled, relaxed, meditative, dreamy,
moody, bittersweet, reflective, introspective, comforting, soothing, steady, rolling,
groovy, bouncy-subtle, cool, classy, smoky, patient, immersive, twilight,
understated, elegant, melancholic, hopeful, tender, serene,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, Deep house kick, 909 kick, soft clap/snare on 2&4, offbeat
closed hi-hat, open-hat lifts, shaker loop, ride cymbal pattern, conga/percussion
loops, rim clicks, tom fills, deep sub bass, rolling bassline, dubby bass pulses,
Rhodes chords, jazz chords, extended 7th/9th chords, organ chords, soft piano
stabs, warm pad stacks, airy pads, mellow chord stabs, filter sweeps, dub delay
throws, reverb tails, vinyl/tape texture, subtle vocal chops,

Vocal Tags:
Soulful female lead vocals, male house vocal, breathy close-mic verse, understated
hook, sampled vocal phrases, chopped vocal loops, gospel-leaning ad-libs, stacked
harmonies, stuttered vocal edits, a cappella breakdown moment, vocal-free drops,
filtered vocal snippets, chant hook outro, doubled hook lines, falsetto accents

Prompt #5051

hats, shaker loop, conga accents, airy pad wash, dub delay throws, female vocals, breathy
close-mic verse, understated hook, long DJ intro/outro, song about choosing peace over
proving a point

Tech House
Tech House blends house groove with techno’s stripped, steely edge. Typically
124–130 BPM four-on-the-floor, drum-machine percussion, rolling bass, and
minimal stabs/FX. The sound incubated in early-’90s UK/London nights like Wiggle
and Mr. C’s The Drop (Terry Francis, Nathan Coles, Eddie Richards).

Genre Tags:
Tech house, Classic tech house, UK tech house, Minimal tech house, Deep tech,
Underground tech house, Ibiza tech house, Tech-bass,

Mood Tags:
Groovy, rolling, hypnotic, driving, punchy, tight, locked-in, minimal, sleek, gritty,
nocturnal, afterhours, tense-build, suspenseful, edgy, funky, bouncy, sweaty-club,
dark-leaning, cheeky, confident, relentless, snappy, focused, euphoric release

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, punchy layered kick, 909 kick, clap on 2&4, tight snare, rim
clicks, offbeat closed hi-hat, open-hat lifts, ride cymbal, shaker loop, swung hats,
shuffled percussion, percussion loops, congas, bongos, tom fills, snare build rolls,
white-noise risers, pitch risers, impact hits, reverse cymbals, rolling bassline, sub
bass, rumble bass, bass stabs, mono drop bass, filter sweeps, short chord stabs,
detuned stab synths, acid-tinged bass wiggle, breakdown bass mute,

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, minimal vocal hook, one-liner hook cue, spoken hype lines,
sampled vocal phrases, chopped vocal loops, pitched vocal chops, filtered vocal
snippets, stuttered vocal edits, reverb-delay throws, whispered spoken line, chant
hook, call-and-response cue, crowd-chant outro, short pop hook vocal

Tropical House
Tropical House is a 2010s deep-house offshoot at 100–120 BPM: 4/4 kick, warm
chords, and beachy “tropical” timbres. The term was coined in 2013 by Thomas Jack,
and the sound was popularized worldwide by Kygo.

Genre Tags:
Tropical house, Trop house, Tropical deep house, Vocal tropical house, Pop tropical
house, Balearic house, Summer house, Chill house

Mood Tags:
Sunny, breezy, warm, laid-back, carefree, feel-good, uplifting, dreamy, romantic,
nostalgic, mellow, glossy, airy, bittersweet, escapist

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, clap on 2&4, offbeat hats, shaker loop, light congas, smooth
sub bass, warm pads, major-key stabs, plucky synth lead, steel drum, marimba, pan
flute, sax hook, acoustic guitar chops, risers, delay throws

Vocal Tags:
Airy female lead, male pop lead, breathy verses, pop hook chorus, harmony stack,
duet harmonies, vocal chops, short ad-libs, falsetto accents

Trance
Trance is a 1990s European EDM style built on 125–150 BPM 4/4, repeating melodic
hooks, arpeggios, long breakdowns, and euphoric peak “drops.” It grew from early
Frankfurt/German scenes (Eye Q/Harthouse; Sven Väth) and later went global via
Paul van Dyk, Tiësto, Armin van Buuren, Ferry Corsten, and Above & Beyond.

Genre Tags:
Trance, Classic trance, Uplifting trance, Progressive trance, Vocal trance, Tech
trance, Acid trance, Goa trance, Psychedelic trance, Hard trance, Anthem trance,
Euro trance,

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, soaring, emotional, ecstatic, blissful, euphoric release, anthemic,
epic, radiant, transcendent, celestial, dreamlike, hypnotic, immersive, expansive,
high-energy, driving, intense, rush, suspenseful, tension-build, dramatic, hopeful,
bittersweet-euphoria, nostalgic, cosmic, glowing, powerful, relentless, melodic, airy,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, 909 kick, clap/snare on 2&4, offbeat closed hi-hat, open-hat
lifts, ride cymbal, snare build rolls, tom build fills, rolling bassline, offbeat bass, sub
bass, sidechain pump, supersaw stack lead, saw chord stacks, detuned pluck lead,
arpeggiated synth, trance-gated pad, lush pad stacks, string pads, bright chord
progression, filter sweeps, white-noise sweeps, pitch risers, uplifter FX, reverse
cymbals, impact hits, breakdown pad wash, acid 303

Vocal Tags:
Ethereal female lead, female soprano lead, male tenor lead, anthemic chorus, long
held notes, emotional harmony stacks, whispered hook, spoken phrase,
reverb-drenched vocals, chopped vocal snippets

Psytrance
Psytrance (psychedelic trance) is a late-’90s Goa/Europe trance offshoot: 125–150
BPM 4/4, offbeat/rolling bass, glitchy synths and long breakdowns into peak
climaxes. Key figures include Hallucinogen, Infected Mushroom, Astrix

Genre Tags:
Psytrance, Psychedelic trance, Psy, Progressive psytrance, Psygressive, Twilight
psytrance, Darkpsy, Forest psytrance, Zenonesque,

Mood Tags:
Hypnotic, trippy, hallucinatory, euphoric, cathartic, transcendent, ritualistic,
shamanic, cosmic, alien, surreal, intense, propulsive, high-energy, frenetic,
mind-melting, kaleidoscopic, suspenseful, explosive, primal, feral, eerie, ominous,
nocturnal, deep, immersive, spiraling, glitchy, psychedelic,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, psy kick, rolling offbeat bass, rolling 16th bassline,
triplet-bass variants, sub bass layer, FM bass, resonant filter bass, acid 303 line,
squelchy lead synth, formant lead, arpeggiated synth, evolving pad drones, layered
synth riffs, glitch percussion fills, tribal percussion, ride, snare build rolls, tom build
fills, white-noise risers, pitch risers, uplifter FX, downlifter FX, reverse cymbals,
impact hits, phaser/flanger sweeps, psychedelic FX sweeps, sample stabs,

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, spoken samples, movie/dialogue snippets, psychedelic whispers,
mantra/chant loops, vocoder phrases, chopped vocal snippets, stutter edits,
pitch-stretched vocals, formant-shifted vox, filtered vocal bits, one-line hook cue,
reverb-delay throws, crowd shout accents, glitch vocal cuts, a cappella breakdown,

Hard Trance
Hard Trance is early-’90s Belgian/German/Dutch rave trance pushed to 140–160+
BPM with hard/pitched kicks, resonant bass, acid riffs, and supersaw leads. It
peaked in the late ’90s; key names include DJ Scot Project, Kai Tracid, Derb.

Genre Tags:
Hard trance, Hardtrance, Classic hard trance, 90s hard trance, Rave trance, German
hard trance, Belgian hard trance, Dutch hard trance, Acid hard trance,

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, ravey, high-energy, driving, relentless, stomping, punchy, explosive,
euphoric, ecstatic, anthemic, triumphant, urgent, gritty, edgy, sweaty club, hypnotic,
intense, cathartic-drop, adrenaline, nocturnal, rebellious, loud

Instrument Tags:
Hard 4/4 kick, downpitched kick, 909 kick, clap/snare on 2&4, offbeat hats, open-hat
lifts, ride cymbal drive, rolling offbeat bass, resonant bass, sidechain pump, 303
acid line, acid stabs, supersaw stack lead, hoover lead, detuned pluck lead,
arpeggiated synth runs, trance-gated pads, snare build rolls, tom build fills,
white-noise risers, pitch risers, impact hits, filter sweeps, long breakdowns,

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental drops, minimal vocal hook, spoken samples, rave shouts, one-liner
drop cue, chopped vocal snippets, filtered vocal bits, female trance hook vocal,
male trance hook vocal, anthemic chorus, long held notes, harmony stacks,
reverb-delay throws

Dubstep
Dubstep is a UK bass style from early-2000s South London, typically 140-150 BPM
with half-time drums, huge sub-bass, syncopated rhythms, and sparse, dubby
atmospheres. Key figures: Skream, Benga, Digital Mystikz and Skrillex

Genre Tags:
Dubstep, UK dubstep, Classic dubstep, Deep dubstep, Brostep, Melodic dubstep,
Riddim, Drumstep,

Mood Tags:
Sub-heavy, dark, ominous, moody, spacious, minimal, meditative, dubby, gritty,
menacing, tense, suspenseful, claustrophobic, hypnotic, sinister, industrial, metallic,
abrasive, chaotic, violent-drop, explosive, aggressive, rebellious, apocalyptic,
futuristic, glitchy, eerie, cold, weighty, angry, anxious, adrenaline

Instrument Tags:
140 BPM grid, half-time snare on 3, syncopated kick patterns, swung hi-hats, triplet
hat bursts, snare build rolls, sub drops, wobble bass, LFO mod bass, FM growls,
vowel/formant bass, reese bass, distorted midrange bass, pitch-bent bass slides,
short minor-key stabs, dark pads, dub delay throws, huge reverb tails, filter sweeps,
noise risers, impact hits, siren FX, glitch percussion, chopped break fills,

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental drops, MC bars, grime MC, ragga toasting, reggae/dub vocal samples,
spoken samples, movie/dialogue snippets, chopped vocal one-shots, pitched vocal
chops, whispered hook lines, call-and-response shouts, crowd chant accents,
distorted vocal growls, female topline hook, auto-tuned hook, ad-lib hype tags

Future Bass
Future Bass is a 2010s EDM style that blends trap/dubstep rhythm with detuned
synth-chord drops driven by heavy sidechain and LFO automation. Commonly
130–160 BPM, it’s linked to pioneers like Rustie, and later by artists like Flume.

Genre Tags:
Future bass, Melodic future bass, Vocal future bass, Pop future bass, Festival future
bass, Trap-future bass,

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, emotional, bittersweet, dreamy, shimmering, sparkly, airy,
radiant, hopeful, romantic, nostalgic, warm, sweet, playful, bouncy, energetic,
punchy, buoyant, triumphant, cathartic, tension-build, explosive-drop, ecstatic,
blissful, cinematic, larger-than-life, melancholic, heartfelt, vibrant, addictive, hooky,

Instrument Tags:
Sidechain pump, punchy kick, 808 sub, trap hat rolls, snare rolls, clap stacks,
detuned supersaw chords, wide chord stacks, 7th/9th chord voicings, bright chord
progressions, LFO filter automation, wobbly synth modulation, pluck lead,
arpeggiated synth runs, pitch-bent leads, FM bass stabs, sub drops, lush pads,
risers, white-noise sweeps, impact hits, vocal chop lead, reverb-delay throws,

Vocal Tags:
Female lead vocals, male lead vocals, pop topline chorus, breathy verses, belted
hook, falsetto accents, harmony stacks, chopped vocal hooks, pitched vocal chops,
vocoder phrases, stuttered vocal edits, ad-lib tags, half-sung hooks, vocal-free
drops,

Trap EDM
Trap EDM (aka EDM trap) is a 2010s festival-bass fusion of trap drum language (808
sub, triplet hat rolls) with EDM build-ups, drops, and aggressive sound design, often
around 140 BPM with a half-time feel. It hit the mainstream via Baauer’s “Harlem
Shake” and was shaped by producers like Flosstradamus, and RL Grime.

Genre Tags:
Trap EDM, EDM trap, Festival trap, Hybrid trap, Trapstep, Acid trap, Twerk trap,
Hard trap, Chill trap,

Mood Tags:
Hype, swagger, brash, rowdy, aggressive, punchy, kinetic, bouncy, head-nod, gritty,
dark, ominous, menacing, tense-build, suspenseful, explosive, chaotic, glitchy,
metallic, futuristic, cinematic, anthemic, triumphant, euphoric-drop, adrenaline,
rebellious, nocturnal, playful, cheeky, confident, ruthless, heavy, in-your-face

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 slides, half-time snare on 3, clap stacks, snare rolls, snare triplets,
rapid hi-hat rolls, triplet hats, skitter hats, syncopated kick, sub drops, reese bass,
distorted bass, FM growls, pitch-bent bass dives, brass stabs, horn hits, orchestral
hits, detuned synth leads, laser synth, siren FX, risers, white-noise sweeps, impact
hits, stop-start drops, break fills, glitch percussion fills, vocal chop lead

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, male rap verse, female rap verse, hype shouts, ad-lib tags,
one-liner drop cue, spoken samples, call-and-response chants, chant hook, melodic
hook vocal, auto-tuned hook, vocal chops, pitched vocal chops, stuttered vocal
edits, crowd chant accents

verse, ad-lib tags, one-liner drop cue, build-drop-build-drop, song about cutting distractions
and leveling up, extended DJ outro

Drum and Bass
Drum and Bass (D&B) is UK-born early-1990s breakbeat dance music, evolved from
rave breakbeat hardcore/jungle. It runs fast (165–185 BPM) with chopped funk
breaks, and heavy bass. key shapers include Goldie, LTJ Bukem, and Noisia

Genre Tags:
Drum and bass, Drum ’n’ bass, DnB, D&B, Jungle, 90s DnB, Dancefloor DnB, Liquid
DnB, Liquid funk, Jump up, Techstep, Neurofunk,

Mood Tags:
Adrenaline, high-energy, driving, propulsive, kinetic, punchy, aggressive, gritty, dark,
ominous, tense, suspenseful, urban, futuristic, metallic, cold, hypnotic, headrush,
chaotic, urgent, sharp, intense, euphoric, uplifting, triumphant, airy, dreamy,
soulful, smooth, melancholic, bittersweet, emotional, cinematic, focused, locked-in,

Instrument Tags:
Amen break chops, classic funk break loops, chopped break edits, 2-step DnB
drums, half-time snare on 3, ghost snares, rapid ride patterns, shuffled hats, tight
kick, reese bass, rolling sub bass, sub drop hits, neuro bass growls, FM bass stabs,
wobble bass, pitch-bent bass dives, atmospheric pads, dark drones, jazzy chord
pads, orchestral stabs, glitch fills, time-stretch FX, filter sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, MC bars, hype shouts, ragga toasting, reggae samples, spoken
samples, movie/dialogue snippets, chopped vocal one-shots, vocal chops hook,
pitched vocal chops, soulful female hook, male hook vocal, airy topline chorus,
harmony stacks, call-and-response chants, reverb-delay throws, stuttered vocal
edits,

Liquid DnB
Liquid DnB is the smooth, soulful side of drum & bass: 170–174 BPM rolling breaks,
warm sub-bass, jazzy chords, pads, and vocal hooks. The term was championed in
1999 by Fabio & Sarah Sandy via Creative Source’s Liquid Funk; later carried by
labels like Hospital and artists like High Contrast, Calibre, Netsky.

Genre Tags:
Liquid DnB, Liquid drum and bass, Liquid funk, Liquid D&B, Liquid rollers

Mood Tags:
Warm, soulful, smooth, lush, airy, dreamy, uplifting, euphoric, hopeful, optimistic,
tender, intimate, romantic, bittersweet, nostalgic, reflective, serene, comforting,
mellow, chilled, gentle, floating, spacious, shimmering, bright, sunny, nightdrive,
cathartic, emotional, groove-heavy, head-nod, blissful, soothing,

Instrument Tags:
2-step DnB drums, rolling breakbeats, subtle Amen chops, ghost snares, shuffled
hats, ride wash, rim clicks, soft snares, rolling sub bass, warm sub, light reese bass,
bass guitar tone, Rhodes chords, jazzy piano, lush pad stacks, string pads, airy
atmospheres, guitar licks, sax hook, vocal chop lead, light sidechain pump, filter
sweeps, risers, impact hits, breakdown pad wash,

Vocal Tags:
Soulful female hook, male hook vocal, airy topline chorus, breathy verses, soft
belted hook, harmony stacks, call-and-response hook, chopped vocal hooks,
pitched vocal chops, sampled phrases, light MC phrases, vocal-free drops

Jump Up DnB
Jump Up DnB is the bouncy, bassline-led strain of drum & bass: usually ~170–176
BPM with tight 2-step drums, big hooky “riff” basslines, and crowd-reactive drops,
often boosted by MC energy. It grew out of early ’90s jungle/hardcore and has
cycled through major waves from the late ’90s into the 2000s and beyond.

Genre Tags:
Jump Up DnB, Jump-Up DnB, Jump Up drum and bass, Jump-up, Jump Up jungle,
Rave DnB

Mood Tags:
Rowdy, hype, bouncy, cheeky, playful, ravey, energetic, high-impact,
hands-in-the-air, party-starting, brash, in-your-face, punchy, relentless, chaotic-fun,
noisy, gurning, gritty, edgy, menacing, dark-leaning, tense-build, explosive,
stomping, sweaty-club, crowd-reactive, hooky, mischievous, euphoric drop, loud,

Instrument Tags:
174 BPM grid, 2-step DnB drums, chopped break edits, snappy snare, ghost snares,
shuffled hats, ride wash, quick fill rolls, stop-start edits, sub bass pressure, rolling
sub, wobble bass hook, mid-bass “yoy” riffs, foghorn bass hits, reese bass stabs,
distorted bass, pitch-bent bass dives, call-and-response bass phrases, minimal
pads, rave stabs, siren FX, risers, white-noise sweeps, impact hits, vinyl stops,

Vocal Tags:
MC bars, hype shouts, crowd chant accents, call-and-response chants, one-liner
drop cue, ragga toasting, spoken samples, movie/dialogue snippets, chopped vocal
one-shots, vocal chops hook, stuttered vocal edits, short hook phrases, vocal-free
drops, reverb-delay throws, doubled ad-libs,

Neurofunk
Neurofunk is a dark,drum & bass style (70–176 BPM) that emerged in London as
techstep evolved: razor-sharp 2-step drums, hyper-detailed modulated reese/neuro
bass, minimal melody, sci-fi tension. Early touchstones: Ed Rush & Optical; later
Noisia, Black Sun Empire; term coined by critic Simon Reynolds

Genre Tags:
Neurofunk, Neuro, Neuro DnB, Neuro drum and bass, Neuro rollers, Techstep,
Dark tech DnB, Industrial DnB,

Mood Tags:
Dark, cold, futuristic, clinical, mechanical, cyberpunk, dystopian, aggressive, intense,
tense, suspenseful, ominous, hostile, gritty, metallic, industrial, razor-sharp, precise,
technical, intricate, hyper-focused, propulsive, adrenaline, sinister, hypnotic,
confrontational, explosive, brutal, feral, claustrophobic, high-tech,

Instrument Tags:
174 BPM grid, 2-step DnB drums, snare on 2&4, tight kick, ghost snares, chopped
break edits, shuffled hats, ride wash, micro drum edits, glitch fills, sub bass
pressure, rolling sub, reese bass layer, modulated reese movement, neuro bass
growls, FM bass, resampled bass, formant filter bass, distortion chains, multiband
compression, bandpass sweeps, automation-heavy bass, pitch-bent bass, metallic
percussion, industrial hits, sci-fi FX sweeps, dark drones, minimal pads

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, spoken samples, movie/dialogue snippets, short drop cues,
robotic vocoder phrases, chopped vocal one-shots, stuttered vocal edits,
pitch-shifted vox, whispered phrases, MC hype shouts, sparse hook phrases,
reverb-delay throws, glitch vocal cuts, crowd chant accents

Jungle
Jungle is early-’90s UK rave breakbeat hardcore fused with Jamaican sound-system
culture: 160–175 BPM chopped funk breaks, deep dubby sub-bass, ragga/dancehall
samples, and frantic syncopation. Pioneers include Fabio & Grooverider, Goldie, LTJ
Bukem, Shy FX; it later crystallized into drum & bass.

Genre Tags:
Jungle, Oldschool jungle, 90s jungle, Jungle techno, Hardcore jungle, Ragga jungle,
Darkcore jungle, Jungle DnB

Mood Tags:
Frenetic, ravey, raw, gritty, bass-heavy, sub-pressure, frantic, syncopated, hyped,
kinetic, chaotic-fun, rebellious, urban, nocturnal, eerie, dark, ominous, tense-build,
suspenseful, explosive, euphoric, hands-in-the-air, sweaty-club, cheeky, playful,
militant, aggressive, edgy, dubby, psychedelic, nostalgic, soulful, uplifting, intense,

Instrument Tags:
Amen break chops, chopped breakbeats, timestretched breaks, break edits, 2-step
jungle drums, ghost snares, shuffled hats, ride wash, snare rush fills, rewind FX, dub
siren, airhorn, ragga vocal samples, reggae bassline, rolling sub bass, reese bass,
wobble bass, dark pads, rave stabs, orchestral stabs, hoover lead, time-stretch FX,
filter sweeps, delay throws, reverb tails, risers, impact hits, vinyl stops,

Vocal Tags:
Ragga toasting, dancehall samples, reggae vocal hooks, MC bars, hype shouts,
call-and-response chants, crowd rewinds, spoken samples, movie/dialogue
snippets, chopped vocal one-shots, pitched vocal chops, stuttered vocal edits,
vocal-free drops, reverb-delay throws, ad-lib tags

Breakbeat
Breakbeat is electronic dance music built from sampled funk & soul drum breaks.
Usually 110–150 BPM, it underpins styles from big beat and nu skool breaks to
jungle. Key pioneers: The Prodigy, The Chemical Brothers, Fatboy Slim.

Genre Tags:
Breakbeat, Breaks, Breakbeat EDM, Breakbeat dance, Funky breaks, Nu skool
breaks, Progressive breaks, Florida breaks, Electro breaks, Acid breaks, Big beat

Mood Tags:
Syncopated, funky, punchy, bouncy, groovy, gritty, raw, crunchy, percussive, kinetic,
high-energy, propulsive, hyped, brash, cheeky, ravey, club-ready, party-starting,
big-drop, explosive, edgy, nocturnal, psychedelic, trippy, futuristic, robotic, glitchy,
stuttery, sleazy, dirty, confident, mischievous, nostalgic, upbeat, sunny, euphoric,

Instrument Tags:
Sampled drum breaks, funk break loops, soul break loops, Amen break chops,
Think break chops, break slicing, break re-arrangement, timestretch breaks, ghost
snares, swung hats, skitter hats, snare rush fills, reverse cymbals, stop-start edits,
vinyl stops, turntable scratches, vinyl crackle, sub bass, rolling bassline, reese bass,
distorted mid-bass, 808-style sub drops, TB-303 acid line, filter sweeps,
phaser/flanger, gated stabs, rave stabs, synth lead riff, vocal chop hits, dub delay
throws, big reverb tails, risers, impact hits, siren FX, FX sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Vocal-free drops, spoken samples, movie/dialogue snippets, chopped vocal
one-shots, stuttered vocal edits, pitched vocal chops, rap verse, MC bars, hype
shouts, call-and-response chants, short hook phrases, ad-lib tags, vocoder phrases,
female hook vocal, male hook vocal, reverb-delay vocal throws

Acid House
Acid House is a mid-80s Chicago house offshoot built on the squelching, resonant
Roland TB-303 bassline. Popularized by Phuture’s 1987 “Acid Tracks” (DJ
Pierre/Spanky/Herb J) and carried to the UK by nights like Shoom, it helped spark
the 1988 rave “Second Summer of Love.”

Genre Tags:
Acid House, Acid, Chicago acid house, 80s acid house, Classic acid, TB-303 house,
303-driven house, Acid rave, UK acid house

Mood Tags:
Psychedelic, trippy, hypnotic, mind-bending, squelchy, twisted, ravey, hedonistic,
ecstatic, euphoric, blissed-out, driving, propulsive, relentless, tense-build, explosive,
frenetic, manic, chaotic-fun, raw, gritty, dirty, sweaty-club, strobe-lit, nocturnal,
rebellious, cheeky, playful, off-kilter, wonky, futuristic, alien, unsettling, ominous,
euphoric-drop, cathartic, addictive-groove, rolling, looped, peak-time,

Instrument Tags:
Roland TB-303 bassline, 303 resonance sweeps, acid squelch, cutoff automation,
accent/slide patterns, step-sequenced riffs, 4-on-the-floor kick, TR-909 kick, 909
open hat offbeat, 909 clap, 909 snare, ride cymbal wash, shuffled percussion, tom
fills, stab chords, rave stabs, sampled vocal hits, delay throws, dubby echoes,
spring-style reverb, filter sweeps, noise risers, impact hits, breakdown bass mute,

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental grooves, short spoken samples, one-liner drop cue, rave shouts,
crowd chants, call-and-response chants, chopped vocal stabs, pitched vocal
snippets, stuttered vocal edits, diva house hook, male hook phrases, ad-lib tags,
vocoder phrases, reverb-delay vocal throws, whispered phrases, MC hype shouts,

Prompt #5801
Acid House, Chicago acid house, 124 BPM, hypnotic, TB-303 bassline, resonance sweeps,
slide accents, TR-909 kick, 909 clap, offbeat open hat, ride wash, stab chords, dub delay
throws, breakdown bass mute, vocal-free groove, song about ditching distractions and
locking in, long DJ outro

UK Garage
UK Garage (UKG) is early–mid ’90s London dance music: sped-up US garage house
(~130 BPM) with swung, shuffled hats, 2-step “skippy” kicks or 4x4, heavy sub-bass,
time-stretched/chopped R&B vocals, and pirate-radio MC culture. Key figures: Todd
Edwards, Tuff Jam, Artful Dodger, MJ Cole, Wookie, So Solid Crew, DJ EZ.

Genre Tags:
UK Garage, UKG, UK garage house, 2-step garage, 2-step, Speed garage, 4x4 garage,
Bassline garage, Organ garage, Deep garage, Garage rollers, NUKG, Future garage,

Mood Tags:
Bouncy, swingy, playful, cheeky, flirty, feel-good, upbeat, glossy, smooth, soulful,
warm, romantic, bittersweet, nostalgic, late-night, nocturnal, urban, slick, confident,
swagger, hype, party-starting, hands-in-the-air, ravey, kinetic, driving, shuffling,
tense-build, dark-leaning, bass-heavy, rowdy, intimate, sentimental

Instrument Tags:
130 BPM swing grid, shuffled hi-hats, offbeat open hats, skippy 2-step kick pattern,
4x4 kick option, snappy claps, crisp snares, rimshot accents, ghost notes, swing
percussion, chopped break touches, sub-bass pressure, sine sub, reese bass stabs,
wobble bass hooks, reverse/warped bass, Korg M1 organ stabs, piano chord stabs,
garage chord pads, string pad lifts, bright rave stabs, pitch-bent bass dives, filter
sweeps, dub delay throws, reverb tails, vocal chop hits, time-stretched vox chops,

Vocal Tags:
Female R&B hook, male R&B hook, soulful diva chorus, smooth male croon,
sing-rap verses, MC bars, hype shouts, call-and-response chants, short hook
phrases, spoken samples, chopped vocal one-shots, time-stretched vocal chops,
pitched vocal snippets, stuttered vocal edits, ad-lib tags, Instrumental drops

Eurodance
Eurodance is late-80s/90s European club-pop built for charts: 4-on-the-floor drum
machines at ~120–150 BPM, bright synth hooks, simple basslines, and verse/chorus
songs often pairing male rap verses with big female choruses. It peaked in the
mid-1990s via acts like 2 Unlimited, Snap!, Culture Beat, and Real McCoy.

Genre Tags:
Eurodance, Classic Eurodance, Euro house, Euroclub, Europop dance

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, ecstatic, celebratory, high-energy, party-starting,
hands-in-the-air, anthemic, triumphant, bright, glossy, bouncy, catchy, playful,
cheeky, flirtatious, romantic, optimistic, feel-good, nostalgic, strobe-lit, nocturnal,
hyper, driving, propulsive, sugary, dramatic, bold, confident, singalong, exuberant,
carefree, over-the-top, hooky, crowd-ready

Instrument Tags:
4-on-the-floor kick, drum machine grooves, TR-909-style kick, clap on 2&4, snare
build rolls, offbeat open hats, ride cymbal wash, syncopated bassline, octave bass
pulses, sub drops, bright synth lead riff, saw lead hook, synth brass stabs, piano
stabs, chord pad stacks, string pad lift, gated stabs, arpeggio runs, risers,
white-noise sweeps, impact hits, key-change lift, breakdown pad wash, vocal chop
hits, DJ intro/outro

Vocal Tags:
Male rap verses, female belted chorus, rap-sung combo, sing-rap verses,
shout-along hook, call-and-response chorus, hype shouts, spoken hook lines, short
vocal stabs, ad-lib tags, harmony stacks, unison singalong chorus, chant accents,
vocoder phrases, crowd callouts, vocal-free drop sections

Jersey Club
Jersey Club is New Jersey’s late-’90s/early-2000s club sound, flipped from Baltimore
club: 130–140 BPM, skippy triplet-kick bounce, huge sub, chopped/looped rap &
R&B vocals, plus signature “bed squeak” hits. Pioneers include DJ Tameil and the
Brick Bandits; later DJ Sliink, Nadus, UNIIQU3 pushed it global

Genre Tags:
Jersey Club, Brick City club, Newark club, Jersey club beats, Jersey club edit,

Mood Tags:
Bouncy, hype, rowdy, playful, cheeky, swagger, brash, aggressive, high-energy,
kinetic, frenetic, chaotic-fun, sweaty-club, hands-in-the-air, propulsive, jumpy,
staccato, gritty, raw, street, nocturnal, party-starting, competitive, crowd-reactive,
mischievous, edgy, tense-build, explosive, viral-dance, chanty

Instrument Tags:
130–140 BPM grid, skippy triplet kick pattern, hard club kicks, snappy claps on 2&4,
rimshot accents, ghost snare flams, swung hats, triplet hat bursts, stop-start
dropouts, sudden silences, chopped break hits, rolling sub bass, 808 sub drops,
reese bass stabs, pitch-bent bass dives, call-and-response drum cuts, chopped R&B
vocal loops, time-stretched samples, stutter edits, vocal one-shots, “bed squeak”
sample hits, airhorn/siren FX, spinback/rewind FX, risers, impact hits,

Vocal Tags:
Chopped vocal hooks, pitched vocal chops, stuttered vocal edits, call-and-response
chants, hype shouts, one-liner drop cues, rap verse, sing-rap hook, short R&B
topline phrases, ad-lib tags, crowd callouts, spoken samples, vocal-free drops,
doubled shout layers, reverb-delay throws

Moombahton
Moombahton is a 2009 D.C.–born club style fusing slowed Dutch/electro house with
reggaeton’s dembow bounce at ~108–115 BPM (“golden tempo”). Big subs, punchy
kicks, chopped vocal loops, and dramatic builds drive the groove. Coined by Dave
Nada after slowing the Afrojack remix of Chuckie & Silvio Ecomo’s “Moombah.”

Genre Tags:
Moombahton, Moombah, Global bass, Latin bass, Dembow EDM, Dutch-house

Mood Tags:
Bouncy, hypnotic, sweaty-club, hedonistic, ravey, playful, cheeky, flirty, rowdy,
bass-heavy, sub-pressure, kinetic, propulsive, swagger, confident, brash, gritty, raw,
edgy, nocturnal, tropical-heat, carnival, party-starting, hands-in-the-air, tense-build,
explosive, manic, chaotic-fun, mischievous, upbeat, euphoric, trippy, percussive,
stompy, rolling, hooky, club-ready, festival-ready, relentless,

Instrument Tags:
108–115 BPM grid, dembow rhythm, reggaeton drum pattern, tresillo accents,
syncopated kick, snappy claps, tight snares, snare build rolls, offbeat hats, shaker
loops, congas, timbales, claves, Latin percussion fills, heavy sub bass, 808 sub
drops, sliding 808s, reese bass stabs, wobble bass accents, Dutch house synth
stabs, saw lead hook, pitch-bent lead, siren/airhorn FX, vocal chop hits,
time-stretched samples, stutter edits, filter sweeps, risers,

Vocal Tags:
Chopped vocal hooks, time-stretched vocal loops, pitched vocal chops, stuttered
vocal edits, Spanish vocal phrases, dancehall samples, ragga toasting, rap verse,
sing-rap hook, female topline hook, male topline hook, call-and-response chants,
hype shouts, one-liner drop cues, spoken samples, vocal stabs,

Nu Disco
Nu Disco is a 2000s-era disco revival: modern house/club production built on
70s–80s disco, boogie and Italo grooves—funk bass/guitar, string stabs, glossy
synth hooks, and looped dancefloor arrangements. Key touchstones include Metro
Area and DFA-era scenes, plus Lindstrøm, Todd Terje and Hercules & Love Affair.

Genre Tags:
Nu Disco, Nu-Disco, Indie Dance, Balearic disco, Scandinavian disco, Synth-disco,
Post-disco revival, Leftfield disco, Club disco,

Mood Tags:
Groovy, funky, upbeat, euphoric, feel-good, celebratory, bright, sunny, playful,
cheeky, flirty, romantic, confident, chic, stylish, retro-future, nostalgic, carefree,
joyful, bouncy, dancefloor, nocturnal, seductive, dreamy, uplifting, smooth, hooky,
cruising, vibrant, glittery,

Instrument Tags:
Disco bass guitar, slapped bass, funk bassline, rhythm guitar chanks, wah guitar,
Nile-style guitar chops, disco drums, four-on-the-floor kick, disco hi-hat groove,
handclaps, tambourine, congas, bongos, cowbell, string stabs, string pad lift, brass
stabs, Rhodes chords, electric piano riffs, synth bass, Moog bass, arpeggiated
synths, bright saw lead hook, analog polysynth chords, vocoder/talkbox synth,
filtered disco samples, tape saturation, sidechain pump,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, falsetto lead, disco diva chorus, soulful hook, airy pop
hook, duet vocals, call-and-response chorus, chant hook, spoken phrases, vocoder
vocals, talkbox vocals, stacked harmonies, ad-lib tags, chopped vocal hooks, vocal
stabs, vocal-free sections

Hardstyle
Hardstyle is a hard-dance EDM style born in the late ’90s/early ’00s, fusing hard
trance and hardcore. It centers on distorted kickdrums and offbeat reverse bass,
typically 150 BPM, with screech leads, huge build-ups, and melodic climaxes.
Popularized by The Prophet, Showtek, Headhunterz.

Genre Tags:
Hardstyle, Hard dance, Early hardstyle, Nustyle, Rawstyle, Melodic hardstyle,
Festival hardstyle, Classic hardstyle,

Mood Tags:
Adrenaline, intense, high-energy, relentless, driving, aggressive, hard-hitting,
punchy, explosive, rave, euphoric, uplifting, anthemic, triumphant, heroic,
emotional, epic, cinematic, dramatic, soaring, powerful, rebellious, gritty, raw, dark,
industrial, brutal, chaotic, hype, immersive,

Instrument Tags:
Distorted hardstyle kick, long kick tail bass tone, kick pitching melody, offbeat
reverse bass, gated kick layers, kickrolls, kick fills, triplet kick rolls, screech lead
synths, detuned saw leads, supersaw anthem lead, hardstyle pluck lead, reese bass
layer, offbeat open hats, ride wash, clap stack, snare build rolls, tom fills, uplifter
risers, white-noise sweeps, downlifters, impact hits, cinematic booms, orchestral
choir pads, climax drop, callout FX, crowd noise lifts

Vocal Tags:
Spoken-word intro, cinematic voiceover, short vocal samples, one-liner hook
phrases, chant hook, crowd chants, hype shouts, male vocal hook, female vocal
hook, pop topline chorus, whispered phrases, stacked harmonies, vocal chops,
vocoder phrases, instrumental drop sections, reverb-delay throws

momentum, extended final climax

Happy Hardcore
Happy Hardcore is a bright, high-BPM offshoot of early ’90s UK rave
breakbeat-hardcore. 160–180 BPM, it layers four-on-the-floor kicks with sped-up
breaks, major-key piano riffs, supersaw hooks, and uplifting, pitched-up vocals. Key
names: Hixxy, DJ Sharkey, Dougal, Slipmatt, Scott Brown, Darren Styles.

Genre Tags:
Happy Hardcore, 4-beat, UK hardcore, 90s happy hardcore, Happy rave, Rave
hardcore, Piano hardcore, Breakbeat hardcore, UK rave, Hardcore rave

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, ecstatic, joyful, feel-good, bright, sunny, bouncy, bubbly, playful,
cheeky, cute, sugary, glittery, anthemic, singalong, hands-in-the-air, triumphant,
sentimental, romantic, yearning, nostalgic, dreamy, carefree, exuberant, hyper,
manic, frenetic, adrenaline, rushy, high energy, relentless, ravey, chaotic, emotional,

Instrument Tags:
160–180 BPM grid, four-on-the-floor kick, distorted 909 kick, long kick tail bass tone,
offbeat open hats, ride wash, clap stack, snare rush fills, snare build rolls, cymbal
crash, breakbeat layers, Amen break chops, timestretched breaks, shuffle
percussion, rolling sub bass, reverse bass, supersaw anthem lead, detuned saw
lead, hoover lead, rave stabs, pitch-bent lead, arpeggiated synth runs, major-key
chord pads, string pad, piano rolls, bright piano stabs, FX sweeps, whistle/horn FX,

Vocal Tags:
Female lead vocals, male lead vocals, pop topline chorus, high-energy hook,
pitched-up vocals, chipmunk vocal chops, time-stretched vocal, chopped vocal,
vocal stabs, crowd chants, hype shouts, spoken samples, sing-rap verses, harmony
stacks, doubled leads, instrumental drop sections

Glitch
Glitch is 1990s experimental electronic (post-digital) that turns malfunctions—CD
skips, digital distortion, bit-rate reduction, clicks/pops, software crashes into
rhythm and texture. It grew via Mille Plateaux/Achim Szepanski and touchstones
like Oval, later linked to microsound minimalism (e.g., Ryoji Ikeda).

Genre Tags:
Glitch, Glitch music, Post-digital, Minimal glitch, Experimental electronic, Laptop
music, Glitch ambient, Glitch techno, Glitch pop, Noise-electronica,

Mood Tags:
Abstract, cerebral, clinical, icy, mechanical, synthetic, digital, fractured, twitchy,
stuttering, jittery, off-kilter, uneasy, ominous, tense, paranoid, alien, futuristic,
hypnotic, minimal, sparse, restrained, meditative, detached, eerie, glitchy, nervous,
anxious, uncanny, unsettled, obsessive, precise, fragmented, disorienting,

Instrument Tags:
Clicks, pops, crackle, static, digital clipping, bitcrush, downsampling, bit-rate
reduction artifacts, buffer stutters, CD-skip loops, circuit-bent noise, micro-edits,
cut-up slices, stutter chops, granular synthesis, glitch percussion, skitter hats,
rimshot ticks, blip percussion, chopped breaks, gate chops, sidechain pumping,
filter automation, spectral processing, time-stretch artifacts, reverse hits, tape-stop
effect, pitch glitches, sine bleeps, noise sweeps, glitch fills, field recordings,

Vocal Tags:
Vocal samples, chopped vocal hooks, stuttered vocal edits, time-stretched vox,
pitched vocal chops, spoken word snippets, robotic TTS phrases, vocoder phrases,
whispered fragments, one-liner samples, glitchy ad-lib tags, callout shouts, heavily
processed vocals, formant-shifted vox, reverb-delay throws, vocal stabs

Prompt #6201

stutters, sine bleeps, sub bass pulses, sparse glitch percussion, spectral smears, reverb
tails, vocal-free, A/B sections, breakdown of pure texture, song about finding calm inside
noise, looped outro

habits like files, hard stop

Glitch Hop
Glitch Hop is midtempo electronic that fuses hip-hop swing and breakbeats with
glitch micro-edits (stutters, bitcrush, buffer repeats) and bass-forward synth design,
often ~80–130 BPM (commonly ~110). It rose in the mid-2000s via artists like The
Glitch Mob, Pretty Lights, Tipper, Opiuo.

Genre Tags:
Glitch Hop, Glitch-hop, Click hop, Blip hop, IDM hip hop, Glitch funk, Bass glitch,
Midtempo bass, Breakbeat glitch, Funky breaks, Leftfield bass

Mood Tags:
Funky, head-nod, bouncy, punchy, tight, crisp, bass-heavy, gritty, edgy, playful,
quirky, weird, off-kilter, twitchy, stuttery, jittery, fractured, chopped, chaotic,
energetic, hype, kinetic, trippy, futuristic, digital, mechanical, quirky, elastic,
unpredictable, intricate, micro-detailed

Instrument Tags:
Swung breakbeat drums, chopped break fragments, hip-hop kick snare, rimshot
ticks, hi-hats, glitch percussion, micro-edits, beat repeat, buffer stutters, stutter
chops, tape-stop hits, bitcrush, granular chops, FFT FX, filter automation, turntable
scratches, sub bass pulses, FM bass growls, reese bass stabs, wobble bass, funky
synth bassline, synth plucks, glitchy arps, vocal chop hits, risers/downlifters

Vocal Tags:
Male rap verse, female rap verse, tight rhythmic flow, laid-back flow, rapid-fire bars,
sing-rap hook, chopped vocal hooks, stuttered vocal edits, pitched vocal chops,
time-stretched vox, vocal one-shots, spoken word snippets, vocoder phrases,
formant-shifted vox, ad-lib tags, call-and-response chants, hype shouts, heavily
processed vocals, instrumental drops

IDM
IDM (Intelligent Dance Music) is early-’90s experimental electronica shaped by
intricate, idiosyncratic beats, and unusual sound design. Key artists include Aphex
Twin, Autechre, Squarepusher, μ-Ziq, The Black Dog, Boards of Canada.

Genre Tags:
IDM, Intelligent Dance Music, Intelligent techno, Braindance, IDM techno, IDM
electronica, Experimental electronica, Glitch IDM, Breakbeat IDM

Mood Tags:
Cerebral, abstract, intricate, precise, micro-detailed, off-kilter, twitchy, jittery,
fractured, playful, quirky, mischievous, uncanny, icy, clinical, mechanical, digital,
futuristic, alien, introspective, tense, paranoid, anxious, ominous, dreamy,
nostalgic, bittersweet, restless, kinetic, chaotic, unpredictable, surreal, otherworldly

Instrument Tags:
Complex breakbeats, chopped breaks, drill-style hats, skitter percussion, glitch fills,
odd-meter accents, polyrhythms, syncopated kicks, micro-edits, stutter chops,
buffer repeats, bitcrush/downsample, time-stretch artifacts, granular chops,
spectral/FFT FX, FM bass growls, reese bass, sine sub pulses, metallic hits,
bleeps/blips, detuned synth leads, arpeggiated synths, pad soundscapes, ambient
chords, field recordings, tape-stop, reverse hits, filter automation,

Vocal Tags:
Male rap verse, female rap verse, tight rhythmic flow, laid-back flow, rapid-fire bars,
sing-rap hook, chopped vocal hooks, stuttered vocal edits, pitched vocal chops,
time-stretched vox, vocal one-shots, spoken word snippets, vocoder phrases,
formant-shifted vox, ad-lib tags, call-and-response chants, hype shouts, heavily
processed vocals, instrumental drops

Chillout Electronic
Chillout Electronic is relaxed electronic music rooted in late-80s UK “chill-out rooms”
and early ambient house. Typically under 120 BPM, it uses slow grooves, sampling,
and blended electronic and acoustic textures for unwinding or DJ cool-downs;
Ibiza’s Café del Mar mixes helped popularize the sound.

Genre Tags:
Chillout electronic, Chillout, Downtempo, Downbeat, Ambient lounge, Electronica

Mood Tags:
Relaxed, calm, serene, soothing, mellow, laid-back, dreamy, hazy, lush, airy,
spacious, gentle, peaceful, tranquil, meditative, introspective, contemplative,
reflective, bittersweet, hopeful, cozy, drifting, floating, hypnotic, slow-burn, sunrise,
blissful, smooth, soft, quiet, restful, weightless, patient, tender, comforting

Instrument Tags:
Slow breakbeats, soft 4/4 kick, downtempo drum groove, brushed snares, rimshot
accents, shakers, congas, bongos, warm sub bass, mellow bassline, Rhodes chords,
electric piano riffs, acoustic guitar, nylon guitar, clean electric guitar, soft synth
pads, analog polysynth chords, gentle arpeggios, string pad lift, dub delay throws,
reverb wash, filter sweeps, vinyl crackle, tape saturation, field recordings, nature
ambience, sampled textures, subtle FX risers,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, instrumental sections, female airy vocals, male soft vocals,
breathy delivery, whispered lines, spoken word snippets, gentle sing-rap, trip-hop
style verses, chopped vocal hooks, vocal pads, harmony stacks, reverb-drenched
vocals, vocoder phrases, minimal ad-libs

Downtempo
Downtempo is slower electronic music, (80–110 BPM) blending groove with
atmospheric, layered textures for chill-out rooms. It overlaps with ambient; key
artists include Massive Attack, Portishead, Tricky, and Nightmares on Wax

Genre Tags:
Downtempo, Downbeat, Chillout, Chill-out, Electronica, Downtempo electronica,

Mood Tags:
Relaxed, mellow, laid-back, calm, serene, soothing, hazy, dreamy, airy, spacious,
lush, smooth, soft, gentle, tranquil, meditative, introspective, reflective,
contemplative, romantic, tender, hopeful, peaceful, comforting, cozy, weightless,
floating, hypnotic, slow-burn, patient, nocturnal, sunrise, blissful, soulful, moody,
smoky, drifting, understated, grounded, earthy, cathartic, healing

Instrument Tags:
Downtempo drum groove, slow breakbeats, soft 4/4 kick, brushed snares, rimshot
accents, ghost notes, shakers, tambourine, congas, bongos, hand percussion, warm
sub bass, mellow bassline, Rhodes chords, electric piano riffs, soft piano stabs,
nylon guitar, clean electric guitar, muted guitar licks, soft synth pads, analog
polysynth chords, gentle arpeggios, filtered chord loops, sampled textures, vinyl
crackle, tape saturation, tape wobble, field recordings, nature ambience, city-night
ambience, spring reverb, reverb wash, filter sweeps, reverse hits, subtle risers,

Vocal Tags:
female airy vocals, male soft vocals, breathy delivery, close-mic intimate vocals,
whispered lines, spoken word snippets, half-sung hooks, gentle sing-rap, trip-hop
style verses, chopped vocal hooks, vocal pads, harmony stacks, reverb-drenched
vocals, vocoder phrases, minimal ad-libs, one-liner samples

Dariacore
Dariacore (aka hyperflip) is an early-2020s internet mashup microgenre popularized
by Jane Remover’s leroy project. Tracks collide pop hooks with Jersey club,
hardstyle, and breakcore, packed with micro-edits, vocal chops, and memes.

Genre Tags:
Dariacore, Hyperflip, Mashup, Mashup EDM, Plunderphonics, Sampledelia

Mood Tags:
Hyperactive, chaotic, manic, unhinged, frantic, adrenaline, explosive, high-voltage,
overstimulated, euphoric, ecstatic, giddy, absurd, goofy, ironic, playful, defiant,
rebellious, aggressive-fun, tense-build, jump-scare drops, whiplash, unpredictable,
surreal, uncanny, glitchy, twitchy, jittery, rattling, ravey, party-crash, nostalgic,
Y2K-brain, sugar-rush, fever-dream, exhilarating

Instrument Tags:
Chopped pop samples, acapella stems, hard cuts, micro-edits, beat repeat, buffer
stutters, stutter chops, time-stretch artifacts, pitch glitches, tape-stop drops, glitch
percussion, skitter hats, snare rush fills, breakbeat chops, DnB-style breaks,
half-time switchups, jersey club kick pattern, triplet hats, bed squeak samples,
airhorns, siren SFX, car-crash SFX, risers, impact hits, sub drops, distorted bass, FM
bass growls, reese bass, hardstyle kick, kick pitching, supersaw bursts, detuned
leads, rave stabs, filter sweeps, abrupt silence gaps

Vocal Tags:
Vocal chops, chopped-hook chorus, pitched-up vocals, chipmunk vox,
formant-shifted vox, time-stretched vox, stuttered vocal edits, callout one-liners,
meme voice clips, spoken samples, robotic TTS phrases, hype shouts, vocal stabs,
rapid-fire sample flips, heavy FX vocals

DnB breakbeat chops, hardstyle kick hits, reese bass, supersaw bursts, airhorn SFX, abrupt
silence gaps, pitched-up vocal chops, chant hook, beat switch mid-song, song about
deleting your old self like a corrupted save file, hard stop

Chiptune
Chiptune is electronic music made by programming the sound chips (PSG/SID/FM)
of vintage game consoles, computers, and arcade boards or emulations. Built from
simple waveforms. Modern acts include Anamanaguchi and Chipzel.

Genre Tags:
Chiptune, 8-bit music, 16-bit, Chipwave, Bitpop, Tracker music, Game Boy music,

Mood Tags:
Nostalgic, playful, upbeat, bright, bouncy, energetic, cheerful, quirky, whimsical,
adventurous, heroic, triumphant, arcade-hype, boss-battle, speedrun, mischievous,
anxious, jittery, twitchy, tense, suspenseful, mysterious, dreamy, bittersweet,
melancholic, cozy, lo-fi, glitchy, digital, pixel-pop, punchy, hyper, frenetic, cathartic,
catchy, singalong, euphoric, determined, wonder

Instrument Tags:
Square wave lead, pulse wave lead, duty-cycle modulation, triangle bass,
noise-channel snare, noise hats, chiptune kick thump, arpeggio chords, rapid
arpeggiator, fast note retrigger, tracker rows/steps, portamento slide, vibrato, pitch
bends, hard panning tricks, tiny sample drums, 4-channel limitation, SID filter
sweeps, NES APU tones, Game Boy PSG tones, FM bass growls, YM2612 FM brass,
bitcrush, downsample artifacts, blip percussion, beep melodies, SFX bleeps, coin-up
hits, laser zaps, glitch fills, tempo ramps

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, chopped vocal samples, tiny 8-bit vocal samples, pitched-up
vocal chops, vocoder vocals, robot voice, TTS phrases, talkbox-style hook, one-liner
samples, chant hook, male vocals, female vocals, stacked doubles, lo-fi pop vocals

Synthwave
Synthwave is a mid-2000s electronic revival that recreates ’80s film/game
soundtrack energy with analog-style synths, drum machines, and big gated reverb.
Popularized by acts like Kavinsky, Perturbator, Carpenter Brut, and The Midnight.

Genre Tags:
Synthwave, Retrowave, Outrun, Futuresynth, Dreamwave, Darksynth, Spacewave

Mood Tags:
Nostalgic, retro-futuristic, neon-lit, nocturnal, cinematic, euphoric, bittersweet,
wistful, dreamy, shimmering, romantic, lonely, hopeful, melancholy, anxious, tense,
ominous, heroic, driving, sleek, glossy, seductive, moody, hypnotic, floaty,

Instrument Tags:
Analog synth leads, sawtooth bass, arpeggiator lines, sequenced bassline,
Juno-style pads, Prophet pads, chorus pads, synth brass stabs, detuned poly
chords, FM bell plucks, DX-style electric piano, LinnDrum kits, TR-707/808 hits,
gated reverb snare, tom fills, clap stacks, sidechain pump, chorus electric guitar,
palm-muted guitar riffs, synth bass slides, risers, VHS/tape noise,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, male vocals, female vocals, breathy lead, crooned baritone,
airy falsetto, synth-pop topline, vocoder lead, talkbox hook, stacked harmonies,
wide doubles, whispered ad-libs, anthem chorus, spoken word intro, choir pads,

Darkwave
Darkwave is the darker, moodier branch of late-’70s/early-’80s post-punk and new
wave , built on minor keys, introspective lyrics, and a blend of guitars + synths +
drum machines. It overlaps with gothic rock; key names include Joy Division, The
Cure, Depeche Mode, Clan of Xymox, Cocteau Twins, Dead Can Dance.

Genre Tags:
Darkwave, Dark wave, Post-punk darkwave, New wave darkwave, Gothic darkwave,
Goth wave, Cold wave, Minimal wave, Ethereal wave, Synth-goth,

Mood Tags:
Brooding, bleak, romantic, sorrowful, melancholic, introspective, nocturnal,
haunted, moody, cold, detached, nihilistic, anxious, ominous, tense, claustrophobic,
paranoid, hypnotic, trancey, somber, forlorn, yearning, dramatic, funereal, eerie,
shadowy, bittersweet, desperate, resigned,

Instrument Tags:
Chorus electric guitar, palm-muted riffing, flanger guitar, reverb-drenched
arpeggios, melodic bass guitar, picked basslines, drum machine grooves, LinnDrum
hits, tight acoustic snare, gated reverb snare, tom fills, cold synth pads, minor-key
synth chords, analog arpeggiators, sequenced bass synth, mono synth lead,
detuned poly synth, chorus pads, sparse piano, string synth, sampler stabs,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, baritone croon, icy monotone, deadpan delivery, gothic
tenor, breathy alto, detached phrasing, talk-sung verses, chant hook, whispered
ad-libs, doubled vocals, harmonized chorus, distant reverb vocal, spoken word
interlude, dramatic sustained notes, low-register melodies

Coldwave
Coldwave (cold wave) is a late-’70s/early-’80s European post-punk offshoot
(France/Belgium/Poland) built on icy minimalism: prominent basslines, dry drum
machines, and affordable analog synths alongside stark guitars. Vocals are
detached and nocturnal. Key acts include KaS Product, Asylum Party, Trisomie 21.

Genre Tags:
Coldwave, Cold wave, La vague froide, European coldwave, French coldwave,
Belgian coldwave, Polish coldwave, Post-punk, Minimal wave, Minimal synth

Mood Tags:
Icy, austere, detached, bleak, nocturnal, urban, paranoid, anxious, somber,
melancholic, tense, claustrophobic, sterile, mechanical, minimal, stark, ghostly,
eerie, brittle, hollow, numb, distant, resigned, brooding, cold-romantic, repressed,
suspenseful, hypnotic, monotone, alienated,

Instrument Tags:
Picked bass guitar, melodic basslines, bass ostinato, chorus bass, chorus electric
guitar, flanger guitar, clean arpeggio guitar, palm-muted riffs, dry drum machine,
rigid kick-snare pattern, rimshot accents, gated snare, tom-machine fills, analog
monosynth bass, sequenced bassline, arpeggiator synth, detuned poly pads,
minor-key synth stabs, string synth, organ synth, sparse piano, tape hiss, short
plate reverb, delay washes,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, baritone croon, icy alto, detached phrasing, deadpan
delivery, monotone melody, talk-sung verses, spoken word lines, clipped diction,
chant hook, call-and-response hook, whispered ad-libs, doubled vocals, distant
reverb vocal, minimal chorus, harmonized chorus

Ethereal Wave
Ethereal Wave is an early-’80s UK offshoot of gothic rock/darkwave, closely tied to
4AD acts like Cocteau Twins, This Mortal Coil, and early Dead Can Dance. It centers
on shimmering, effects-heavy guitars, lush synth pads, drum machines, and
romantic otherworldly haze, overlapping dream pop/shoegaze.

Genre Tags:
Ethereal wave, Ethereal darkwave, Ethereal goth, Ethereal pop, 4AD-era ethereal,
Darkwave,

Mood Tags:
Otherworldly, romantic, dreamy, wistful, bittersweet, melancholic, yearning, tender,
fragile, nocturnal, moonlit, misty, hazy, floating, trancey, hypnotic, meditative,
introspective, sacred, reverent, angelic, haunted, mournful, soft-focus, shimmering,
distant, weightless, luminous, intimate, surreal,

Instrument Tags:
Reverb-drenched guitars, chorus guitar, delay guitar arpeggios, shimmer reverb,
flanger textures, volume-swells, tremolo guitar, layered guitar soundscapes,
melodic bass guitar, fretless bass, post-punk basslines, drum machine grooves,
LinnDrum-style hits, tight kick-snare, gated snare accents, rimshot ticks, synth pad
haze, analog polysynth chords, detuned pads, string synth, choir pads, arpeggiator
sparkle, bell-like synth plucks, tape hiss, long reverb tails,

Vocal Tags:
Female vocals, male vocals, soprano lead, airy falsetto, breathy alto, baritone croon,
wordless vocals, glossolalia-style phrasing, soft vibrato, distant reverb vocal, wide
doubles, stacked harmonies, choral layers, whispered ad-libs, hymn-like chorus,
slow-bloom hook, intimate close-mic verses

Dark Ambient
Dark Ambient is a mid-1980s post-industrial offshoot of ambient built from
ominous drones, discordant overtones, and beatless or minimal-beat soundscapes.
It uses synths, eerie samples, treated guitars, noise, and field recordings to conjure
vast, unsettling spaces; key figures include Lustmord and Coil.

Genre Tags:
Dark ambient, Ambient industrial, Industrial ambient, Isolationist ambient, Ritual
ambient, Black ambient, Drone ambient, Dark drone, Soundscape ambient

Mood Tags:
Ominous, foreboding, unsettling, eerie, dread, oppressive, claustrophobic,
suffocating, bleak, desolate, hollow, cold, glacial, nocturnal, subterranean,
catacomb, cavernous, monolithic, abyssal, apocalyptic, paranoid, tense,
suspenseful, ritualistic, funereal, spectral, ghostly, dissonant, hypnotic, trancey,
meditative, vast, isolating, nihilistic,

Instrument Tags:
Sub-bass drones, analog synth drones, modular synth, granular synthesis, noise
beds, distortion, feedback drones, guitar textures, bowed metal, metallic scrapes,
industrial impacts, distant booms, reversed swells, atonal pads, choir pads, tape
hiss, vinyl crackle, field recordings, cave ambience, wind ambience, machine-room
ambience, convolution reverb, long reverb tails, delay washes, spectral resonators,
sampled gongs, ritual percussion, low-frequency rumbles,

Vocal Tags:
Female vocals, male vocals, choral layers, airy falsetto, breathy alto, baritone croon,
wordless vocals, glossolalia-style phrasing, soft vibrato, distant reverb vocal

Drone Ambient
Drone Ambient is ambient built from sustained drones—long tones or tone clusters
that evolve slowly with little or no rhythm. It emphasizes gradual timbral shifts,
deep reverb, and immersion; key artists include Éliane Radigue and Stars of the Lid.

Genre Tags:
Drone ambient, Ambient drone, Drone, Drone music, Minimal ambient, Longform
ambient, Experimental ambient, Microtonal drone

Mood Tags:
Meditative, hypnotic, suspended, timeless, patient, vast, spacious, weightless,
serene, tranquil, contemplative, introspective, immersive, slow-burning, foggy,
hazy, distant, reverent, otherworldly, minimal, austere, glacial, numb, melancholy,
somber, eerie, uncanny, spectral, cathartic, monolithic,

Instrument Tags:
Sustained synth drones, modular synth patches, analog oscillators, sine
sub-drones, slow filter sweeps, microtonal detune, beating tones, granular smear,
noise bed, tape loops, reel-to-reel hiss, time-stretched textures, convolution reverb,
long delay feedback, Ebow guitar drones, volume-swells, bowed guitar, pedal
sustain, harmonium, pipe organ drones, shruti box, tanpura-style drone, string
ensemble pads, field recordings, room tone,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, wordless vocals, distant choir layers, humming drones,
overtone singing, whispered phrases, spoken word fragments, heavily reverbed
vocals, vocoder drone, chanted syllables, chopped vocal textures, breathy exhale
samples, choir pad “ahhs,”

song about staying awake in a server-room blackout, fade to air tone

Space Ambient
Space Ambient is tranquil, hypnotic ambient designed to evoke vast spatial
imagery—floating, cruising, inner/outer space—via long synth pads, sequenced
arpeggios, drones, and huge reverb. It traces to 1970s Berlin School synth pioneers
and later new-age/planetarium listening culture.

Genre Tags:
Space ambient, Space music, Cosmic ambient, Deep space ambient, Space
soundscapes, Cinematic space ambient, Spacewave, Interstellar ambient

Mood Tags:
Expansive, vast, infinite, weightless, floating, drifting, transportive, hypnotic,
trancey, meditative, contemplative, serene, tranquil, peaceful, soothing, awe,
wonder, sublime, luminous, celestial, cosmic, starbound, mysterious, enigmatic,
distant, detached, dreamy, ethereal, otherworldly, futuristic, glacial, slow-burn,
introspective,

Instrument Tags:
Analog polysynth pads, modular synth drones, sequencer patterns, arpeggiator
lines, resonant filter sweeps, sub-bass drones, string synth, choir pads, bell-like
synth plucks, theremin-like lead, soft FM tones, granular textures, noise beds, tape
hiss, field recordings, stereo imaging, long delay feedback, huge convolution reverb,
slow LFO drift, sparse pulse hits,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, wordless choir layers, distant vocal pads, vocoder phrases,
whispered vocal samples, spoken word fragments, heavily reverbed vocals,
chopped vocal textures, minimal vocals, no vocals

New Age
New Age is soothing, mood-lifting music for relaxation. Ambient synth pads and
gentle acoustic/world instruments, often with environmental sound effects. It
emerged in late-’60s/’70s US/Europe and became a major category (e.g., Windham
Hill), with artists like Kitaro, Enya, and Andreas Vollenweider.

Genre Tags:
New Age, New-age, New age music, New age instrumental, Contemporary
instrumental, New age ambient, Healing music,

Mood Tags:
Calm, serene, tranquil, soothing, peaceful, restorative, healing, meditative, mindful,
spiritual, uplifting, optimistic, hopeful, blissful, gentle, soft, warm, comforting,
luminous, airy, floating, expansive, spacious, celestial, dreamlike, ethereal, pastoral,
nature-forward, reflective, introspective, tender, slow-burn, minimal, cleansing,
reassuring,

Instrument Tags:
Analog synth pads, digital synth pads, modular drones, soft arpeggiators,
sequenced patterns, warm sub drones, acoustic piano, electric piano, harp,
fingerstyle acoustic guitar, nylon-string guitar, flute, pan flute, Native American
flute, cello, string ensemble pads, choir pads, bell-like synth plucks, wind chimes,
Tibetan singing bowls, gongs, soft mallets, shakers, frame drum, tabla, ocean
waves, rain ambience, birdsong, wind ambience, long reverb tails, delay washes,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, wordless vocals, humming drones, choral “ahhs,” mantra
chant, overtone singing, spoken word meditation, whispered affirmations, heavily
reverbed vocals, airy doubles, vocoder phrases, layered harmonies,

Prompt #6901

soft sub drone, gentle shaker, bell-like plucks, long reverb tails, slow crescendo, A-B-A form,
no vocals, song about starting over one small habit at a time, ambient coda

Celtic New Age
Celtic New Age fuses Celtic themes, instruments, and melodies with New Age’s lush,
relaxation-forward production—airy synth pads, big reverb, gentle rhythms, and
nature-leaning ambience. It crossed into the mainstream in the late 1980s via
Enya/Clannad and stayed popular through the 1990s (e.g., Loreena McKennitt).

Genre Tags:
Celtic New Age, Celtic new-age, Celtic fusion, Celtic ambient, Neo-Celtic,
Contemporary Celtic, Celtic instrumental, Celtic meditation, Irish new age

Mood Tags:
Serene, tranquil, soothing, gentle, warm, comforting, healing, meditative, mindful,
spiritual, reverent, sacred, pastoral, woodland, misty, enchanted, mystical, dreamy,
ethereal, airy, luminous, hopeful, uplifting, tender, romantic, wistful, bittersweet,
nostalgic, calm-focus, floating, expansive, oceanic, twilight, dawn-lit, cathartic,

Instrument Tags:
Celtic harp, lever harp, tin whistle, low whistle, uilleann pipes, fiddle, bodhrán,
frame drum, soft hand percussion, shakers, wind chimes, dulcimer, bouzouki,
mandolin, fingerstyle acoustic guitar, nylon-string guitar, piano, string ensemble
pads, choir pads, warm synth pads, string synth, soft arpeggiators, sub drones, long
delay washes, long reverb tails, ocean/wind/bird ambience,

Vocal Tags:
Female vocals, male vocals, breathy soprano, airy falsetto, gentle baritone, choral
“ahhs,” wordless vocals, layered harmonies, stacked choir layers, Gaelic lyrics, soft
vibrato, distant reverbed vocals, whispered ad-libs, mantra chant, mostly
instrumental, no vocals,

Meditation Music
Meditation Music is low-arousal audio for mindfulness: slow or beatless drones,
gentle harmonies, and minimal sudden changes, often paired with nature
ambience. It ranges from ambient/New Age beds to sound baths (singing bowls,
gongs) and brainwave-entrainment tones like binaural beats.

Genre Tags:
Meditation music, Deep meditation, Guided meditation music, Sound meditation,

Mood Tags:
Calm, serene, tranquil, soothing, peaceful, grounded, centered, present, mindful,
still, quiet, spacious, weightless, floating, gentle, soft, warm, comforting, safe,
restorative, healing, cleansing, tender, patient, slow, minimal, spacious-focus,
meditative, contemplative, introspective, unhurried, sleepy, drowsy, lucid, clear,
open, balanced, luminous, airy, zen, hush,

Instrument Tags:
Sustained synth drones, warm pad haze, sub-bass drone, slow filter sweeps, long
reverb tails, long delay washes, soft arpeggiators, gentle pulse tones, singing bowls,
crystal bowls, gongs, wind chimes, bells, tuning forks, handpan, harmonium,
tanpura-style drone, shruti box, bamboo flute, Native American flute, harp
harmonics, soft piano tones, rain ambience, ocean waves, birdsong, wind
ambience, white noise, pink noise, binaural beat tones, isochronic pulses,

Yoga Music
Yoga Music is intentionally paced music for yoga practice/classes—ambient,
downtempo, or world/neo-spiritual—picked to support breath, pacing, and
relaxation with minimal lyrical distraction; it may include sound-bath tones
(bowls/gongs) or mantra/kirtan chanting.

Genre Tags:
Yoga music, Yoga class music, Yoga flow music, Vinyasa playlist, Yin yoga music,
Restorative yoga music, Savasana music, Breathwork music, Mindfulness music,

Mood Tags:
Calm, serene, tranquil, soothing, peaceful, grounded, centered, mindful, present,
steady, flowing, gentle, soft, warm, comforting, safe, restorative, healing, cleansing,
open, spacious, airy, weightless, floating, expansive, slow, patient, unhurried,
minimal, quiet, tender, reflective, introspective, devotional, reverent, sacred,
luminous, uplifting, hopeful, reassuring,

Instrument Tags:
Warm synth pads, sustained drones, sub-bass drone, slow filter sweeps, soft
arpeggiators, gentle pulse tones, handpan, harmonium, tanpura-style drone, shruti
box, singing bowls, crystal bowls, gongs, tuning forks, wind chimes, bells, soft mallet
hits, frame drum, bodhrán, tabla, shakers, bamboo flute, Native American flute,
harp, acoustic piano, electric piano, nylon-string guitar, fingerstyle acoustic guitar,
string pads, choir pads, ocean waves, rain ambience, forest birds, wind ambience,

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental, Calm narrator, Guided meditation voice, Breath cues, Mantra chant,
Sanskrit lyrics, Humming drones, Choral “ahhs,” Overtone singing, Heavily reverbed
vocals, Distant vocal pads,

Spa Music
Spa Music is low-distraction background music for massage and wellness spaces:
slow, steady, low-dynamic instrumentals (ambient/new age), minimal or no lyrics,
few abrupt changes, often blended with nature sounds and soft mallet/hand
percussion; popular wellness artists include Deuter and Liquid Mind.

Genre Tags:
Spa music, Massage music, Relaxation music, Zen music, Healing music

Mood Tags:
Calm, serene, tranquil, soothing, peaceful, restorative, healing, gentle, soft, warm,
comforting, safe, grounded, centered, mindful, present, slow, minimal, spacious,
airy, weightless, floating, light, luminous, tender, reassuring, balanced, clean, pure,
quiet, hush, relaxed-focus, meditative, dreamy, spa-luxe, refreshed, mellow,

Instrument Tags:
Warm synth pads, sustained drones, sub-bass drone, slow filter sweeps, soft
arpeggiators, acoustic piano, electric piano, harp, fingerstyle acoustic guitar,
nylon-string guitar, handpan, kalimba, bamboo flute, Native American flute, singing
bowls, crystal bowls, gongs, wind chimes, bells, tuning forks, soft mallet hits,
shakers, frame drum (soft), light tabla, ocean waves ambience, rain ambience,
stream-water ambience, forest birds, wind ambience, pink/white noise bed, long
reverb tails, delay washes, loopable transitions, gentle dynamics,

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental, Wordless vocals, Humming drones, Choral “ahhs,” Distant vocal pads,
Whispered affirmations (very soft), Calm narrator (minimal), Spoken word
intention-setting, Heavily reverbed vocals, Minimal vocal samples, Mantra chant,

Lounge
Lounge is mid-century easy listening for cocktail-bar ambiance: scaled-down
swing/big band lushness with touches of exotica and space-age pop. Expect relaxed
tempos, vibraphone/organ, muted brass, Latin percussion, and crooner vocals;
modern usage also includes retro revivals and downtempo ‘lounge’ compilations.

Genre Tags:
Lounge, Lounge music, Cocktail music, Cocktail jazz, Easy listening, Light jazz,

Mood Tags:
Suave, sophisticated, relaxed, breezy, mellow, smooth, elegant, glamorous, refined,
playful, flirtatious, cheeky, kitschy, retro, nostalgic, sun-drenched, tropical, escapist,
swanky, loungey, after-hours, nocturnal, cool, laid-back, warm, cozy, lightly
psychedelic, whimsical, cinematic, postcard-pretty,

Instrument Tags:
Vibraphone, marimba, brushed drum kit, light drum kit, bongos, congas, shakers,
claves, woodblock, timpani swells, upright bass, electric bass, jazz guitar,
nylon-string guitar, tremolo guitar, piano, electric piano, Hammond organ,
Wurlitzer/organ combo, muted trumpet, flugelhorn, saxophone, trombone, string
section, harp gliss, theremin-like lead, space echo delay, spring reverb, stereo
panning, tape saturation,

Vocal Tags:
Male crooner, female smoky vocals, baritone lead, breathy alto, close-mic intimate
vocal, velvety delivery, lounge duet, call-and-response hook, spoken-word
interjections, scat singing, wordless “ooh/ahh” layers, stacked harmonies, heavy
reverb vocals, background vocal ooze, mostly instrumental, no vocals,

Smooth Jazz
Smooth Jazz is a radio-friendly crossover jazz that grew from fusion and soul/R&B in
the late 1970s–1990s. It favors steady grooves, lush synth layers, and singable
melodies over extended solos, often led by soprano/alto sax or trumpet/flugelhorn.

Genre Tags:
Smooth jazz, Contemporary jazz, Crossover jazz, Adult contemporary jazz,

Mood Tags:
Laid-back, silky, polished, glossy, sleek, smooth, mellow, warm, romantic, intimate,
sensual, breezy, easygoing, refined, upscale, sophisticated, relaxed, calm, cozy,
sunny, optimistic, feel-good, gentle, airy, buoyant, light, clean, uncluttered, tasteful,
classy, after-hours, nocturnal, dreamy, soothing, mellow-groove, confident, cool,

Instrument Tags:
Soprano sax lead, alto sax lead, tenor sax lines, sax melody hooks, muted trumpet,
flugelhorn, trumpet lead, smooth electric guitar, clean chorus guitar, jazz guitar,
wah-guitar accents, Rhodes electric piano, Wurlitzer keys, glossy synth pads, digital
synth layers, synth bell plucks, synth brass stabs, synth bass, fretless bass, electric
bass groove, tight drum kit, programmed drums, crisp snare, syncopated hi-hats,
light funk groove, congas, bongos, shakers, tambourine accents, string pads,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, No vocals, Male vocals, Female vocals, Smooth tenor, Soft
baritone, Breathier alto, R&B-inflected singing, gentle melisma, intimate close-mic
vocal, airy doubles, stacked harmonies, soft ad-libs, half-sung hook, spoken
interjections, light scat phrases, reverbed backing vocals,

Nu Jazz
Nu Jazz fuses jazz harmony, improvisation, and live instruments with electronic
production—breakbeats, house grooves, sampling, and synth textures. Emerging in
the 1990s club era, it ranges from downtempo to IDM, and techno-leaning hybrids.

Genre Tags:
Nu jazz, Nü jazz, Nu-jazz, Jazztronica, Future jazz, Electro-jazz, Electronic jazz,

Mood Tags:
Sleek, urbane, cosmopolitan, nocturnal, cool, smooth, polished, glossy, refined,
sophisticated, laid-back, mellow, warm, soulful, groovy, pocketed, hypnotic, trancey,
hazy, dreamy, smoky, sensual, seductive, minimal, spacious, deep, dubby, off-kilter,
syncopated, broken-rhythm, playful, quirky, cerebral, futuristic, kinetic,

Instrument Tags:
Rhodes electric piano, Wurlitzer keys, jazz piano voicings, upright bass, electric bass
groove, fretless bass, live drum kit, brushed drums, breakbeats, broken beat
drums, swung hi-hats, MPC-style sampling, chopped jazz chords, vinyl crackle,
turntable scratches, synth bass, analog polysynth pads, filtered loops, tape delay,
dub reverb, sidechain pump, vibraphone, jazz guitar comping, clean chorus guitar,
saxophone lead, trumpet/flugelhorn lead, muted trumpet fills, string pads,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, No vocals, Spoken word snippets, Soft talk-sung lines, Female
soulful lead, Male smooth tenor, Breathier alto, Jazz scat accents, Wordless
“ooh/ahh” layers, Chopped vocal samples, Vocal chops, Vocoder phrases, Rap
verses, Ad-lib textures, Distant reverbed vocals, Double-tracked hooks

Jazz Fusion
Jazz Fusion blends jazz harmony/improvisation with rock, funk, and R&B grooves,
leaning on electric guitars/bass, amps, synth keys, and driving drums. It emerged in
the late 1960s and was popularized by Miles Davis, later shaped by bands like
Weather Report, Mahavishnu Orchestra, and Return to Forever.

Genre Tags:
Jazz Fusion, Fusion, Jazz-rock fusion, Jazz rock, Electric jazz, Jazz-funk fusion

Mood Tags:
High-energy, adventurous, virtuosic, explosive, intense, driving, aggressive, bold,
electrified, futuristic, sleek, flashy, kinetic, complex, angular, off-kilter, syncopated,
polyrhythmic, odd-meter, technical, tight, punchy, muscular, gritty, raw, cosmic,
psychedelic-tinged, tense, suspenseful, cinematic, triumphant, swaggering, playful,
restless, turbulent, ecstatic, jammy, freewheeling, unpredictable, precision-groove,

Instrument Tags:
Electric guitar lead, distortion guitar, overdriven guitar, wah guitar, fast alternate
picking, fusion guitar runs, clean chorus guitar, octave guitar lines, synth bass,
Moog lead, analog polysynth pads, clavinet funk, Fender Rhodes, Wurlitzer keys,
electric piano comping, Hammond organ, synth brass stabs, jazz piano clusters,
fretless bass slides, slap bass, electric bass pocket, tight drum kit, snare ghost
notes, tom fills, cymbal work, odd-meter grooves, polyrhythms, percussion breaks,
congas, bongos, shakers, saxophone solos, trumpet/flugelhorn lines, violin/strings

Vocal Tags:
Wordless vocals, Scat singing, Soulful male vocals, Soulful female vocals, Funk vocal
hook, Talkbox vocals, Vocoder phrases, Spoken interjections, Double-tracked
hooks, Background “ooh/ahh” layers, Reverb-drenched backing vocals,

Bebop
Bebop is 1940s small-group modern jazz: very fast tempos, intricate heads, rapid
chord changes and advanced harmony (substitutions, ii-V lines), with virtuosic
improvisation and interactive rhythm sections. Key architects include Charlie
Parker, Dizzy Gillespie, Thelonious Monk, Bud Powell, Kenny Clarke, and Max Roach.

Genre Tags:
Bebop, Bop, Modern jazz, 1940s bebop, NYC bebop, Small-group jazz, Combo jazz,

Mood Tags:
High-energy, intense, virtuosic, electrifying, restless, urgent, kinetic, driving, sharp,
angular, spiky, acrobatic, competitive, combative, fearless, cerebral, sophisticated,
witty, playful, cheeky, sly, cool-headed, focused, relentless, nimble, quicksilver,
complex, intricate, unpredictable, daring, combustible, edgy, streetwise,
after-hours, nocturnal, smoky, bustling,

Instrument Tags:
Alto sax lead, Trumpet lead, Tenor sax solos, Bebop piano comping, Acoustic bass
walking lines, Tight drum kit, Ride cymbal timekeeping, “Dropping bombs” on
snare/bass drum, Fast swing feel, Double-time lines, Syncopated accents, Unison
horn heads, Rapid chord changes, ii-V-I chains, Turnarounds, Tritone substitution,
Altered dominants, Chromatic notes, Enclosures, Bebop scales, Passing diminished
chords, Substitute chords, Trading fours, Jagged melodic intervals,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, No vocals, Scat singing, Bebop scat syllables, Vocalese (fast),

Big Band
Big Band is a large jazz dance ensemble—often 17 players—built around sax,
trumpet, and trombone sections plus a rhythm section. In the 1930s–40s Swing Era
it thrived on written arrangements: riffs, shout choruses, and featured solos

Genre Tags:
Big Band, Big band jazz, Swing, Swing era, Swing band, Dance band, Big band swing

Mood Tags:
Swinging, upbeat, energetic, lively, danceable, joyful, celebratory, brassy, bold,
punchy, driving, propulsive, confident, classy, sophisticated, glamorous, suave,
playful, cheeky, flirty, bright, sunny, warm, nostalgic, vintage, retro, bustling,
city-night, after-hours, smoky, electric, triumphant, grand, cinematic, dramatic,
regal, romantic, sentimental, smooth, elegant, cool,

Instrument Tags:
Sax section (2 altos, 2 tenors, baritone), trumpet section, trombone section, rhythm
section (piano, guitar, upright bass, drum kit), walking bass, Freddie Green-style
guitar comping, swing ride cymbal pattern, hi-hat on 2&4, brushes-to-sticks lift,
brass stabs, brass falls/doits, muted trumpets, plunger trombone, sax soli, reed
pads, ensemble hits, stop-time breaks, turnarounds, big band intros/endings,
clarinet doubles, flute doubles, vibraphone, percussion accents

Vocal Tags:
Male crooner, Female jazz vocal, Big band lead singer, Smooth baritone, Bright
tenor, Smoky alto, Belted swing chorus, Call-and-response with band, Scat singing,
Vocalese (fast), Close-mic intimate vocal, Vintage radio vocal tone, Section
“ooh/ahh” backing, Group shout accents, Spoken bandleader cues, Reverb-light
vocals,
