Funk
Funk is groove-first urban dance music from African-American communities, driven
by hard syncopated bass + drums, interlocking riffs, popularized by James Brown.

Genre Tags:
Funk, Classic funk, Deep funk, Soul funk, R&B funk, Jazz-funk, Funk rock, Psychedelic
funk, P-funk, Disco-funk, Boogie funk,

Mood Tags:
Groovy, funky, sweaty, raw, gritty, swaggering, brash, bold, punchy, percussive,
tight, locked-in, pocketed, syncopated, hypnotic, head-nod, dancefloor, party,
playful, cheeky, flirtatious, sexy, dirty, rebellious, defiant, streetwise, high-energy,
explosive, electric, euphoric, feel-good, triumphant, confident, loud-proud,
mischievous, unfiltered,

Instrument Tags:
Syncopated electric bass, slap bass, popping bass, muted bass thump, tight drum
kit, snare ghost notes, “on-the-one” accents, 16th-note hi-hats, kick-drum
syncopation, rimshot backbeats, percussion breaks, congas, bongos, cowbell,
shakers, clavinet riff, Fender Rhodes chords, Wurlitzer stabs, Hammond organ,
chicken-scratch rhythm guitar, muted funk guitar, wah guitar, octave guitar lines,
horn section stabs, trumpet doits, trombone falls, sax riffs, talkbox, synth bass

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, gritty shouts, raspy lead, soulful lead, falsetto hooks,
chant hook, call-and-response with band, hype ad-libs, spoken interjections,
rhythmic talk-singing, tight unison phrases, stacked harmonies, group shout
accents, short sung hooks, minimal lyrics, mostly instrumental, no vocals, rap
bridge, reverb-light vocals, double-tracked hook

Disco
Disco is 1970s club dance music built on a steady four-on-the-floor kick, syncopated
bass, bright orchestration, and synths. DJs and 12-inch extended singles shaped its
long mixes; it rose from Black/Latino/gay nightlife into global pop

Genre Tags:
Disco, Classic disco, 1970s disco, Club disco, Philly disco, Salsoul disco, Studio disco,
Disco-funk, Eurodisco, Italo disco, Space disco, Cosmic disco, Hi-NRG, Post-disco,
Disco revival, Nu-disco, Dance-pop, Electronic disco,

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, celebratory, glamorous, glittery, high-energy, uplifting, feel-good, joyful,
flirty, romantic, sexy, confident, bold, lush, silky, polished, shiny, sparkling,
infectious, propulsive, driving, hypnotic, liberated, ecstatic, playful, cheeky, sunny,
after-hours, triumphant, carefree, sweaty, party, dancefloor, radiant, unfiltered,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, open hi-hat offbeats, 16th-note hi-hats, handclaps,
tambourine lifts, congas, bongos, cowbell, syncopated bassline, octave bass, bass
pops, rhythmic guitar chanks, wah guitar, clean chorus guitar, clavinet accents,
Fender Rhodes, electric piano stabs, disco string section, sweeping strings, brass
stabs, horn riffs, flute fills, synth pads, synth bass, synth-string layers,

Vocal Tags:
Female diva vocals, male falsetto lead, soulful lead, chant hook, call-and-response,
group shouts, stacked harmonies, backing “ooh/ahh”, spoken ad-libs, talk-sung
lines, breathy alto, bright tenor, close-mic vocal, reverb/echo vocal, vamping outro
vocals,

Reggaeton
Reggaeton is Afro-diasporic Latin urban club music that formed in Panama and
Puerto Rico, built on the looping “dembow” rhythm from Jamaican dancehall, fused
with hip-hop and reggae en español. Tempos sit around ~85–105 BPM.

Genre Tags:
Reggaeton, Old school reggaeton, Classic reggaeton, Underground reggaeton, Latin
urban, Perreo, Sandungueo, Maleanteo, Pop reggaeton, Romantic reggaeton,
Neoperreo, Reggaeton duro,

Mood Tags:
Hot, sweaty, sensual, provocative, brash, cocky, confident, dominant, aggressive,
streetwise, gritty, raw, rebellious, defiant, hyped, energetic, club-ready, dancefloor,
bouncy, infectious, flirtatious, playful, cheeky, bold, celebratory, euphoric,
triumphant, nocturnal, after-hours, dangerous, tense, addictive, relentless,

Instrument Tags:
Dembow drum loop, reggaeton kick, snare/clap on 2&4, syncopated percussion,
16th-note hats, hat rolls, dembow fills, 808 sub bass, 808 slides, bass drops, siren
FX, risers, impact hits, synth plucks, marimba/pluck leads, minor-key synth chords,
pad swells, piano stabs, brass stabs, vocal chops, filtered intro, breakdown drop,
DJ-style outro loop, congas, timbales accents, guiro, shakers, cowbell,

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish rap, female Spanish rap, sing-rap hook, melodic chorus, Auto-Tune
lead, gritty delivery, aggressive cadence, perreo chant hook, call-and-response,
ad-lib heavy, hype shouts, whispered bars, triplet flow moments, staccato phrasing,
double-tracked hook, stacked harmonies, Spanglish phrases

sub bass, kick accents, minor-key synth chords, siren FX, vocal chops, filtered intro, drop,
hook-repeat outro, male Spanish rap, aggressive cadence, ad-lib heavy, song about cutting
off fake friends at the club

Dembow
Dembow is Dominican Republic club music built on a sped-up dembow rhythm. An
Afro-Caribbean dancehall loop. Driven by rapid, repetitive rap chants, hard 808
subs, and punchy percussion, often 115–140 BPM

Genre Tags:
Dembow, Dominican dembow, Dembow dominicano, Dominican urbano,
Dominican street club, Caribbean club, Latin urban, Dancehall-derived,
Reggaeton-adjacent, Sped-up dembow, Dembow riddim feel

Mood Tags:
Explosive, high-energy, rowdy, hype, aggressive, brash, cocky, swaggering,
streetwise, gritty, raw, relentless, adrenaline, bouncy, infectious, chaotic, wild,
playful, provocative, sweaty, club, dancefloor, nocturnal, after-hours, dangerous,
tense, fierce, dominant, in-your-face, repetitive-trance, manic, triumphant,

Instrument Tags:
Sped-up dembow drum loop, kick on-the-floor accents, sharp snare/clap on 2&4,
rimshot pops, rapid 16th hats, hat rolls, dembow fills, percussion breaks, conga
splashes, güira ticks, timbales accents, shakers, cowbell, 808 sub bass, distorted
808, 808 slides, bass drops, impact hits, risers, siren FX, airhorn FX (sparingly), synth
plucks, marimba/pluck leads, minor-key synth chords, staccato synth stabs, pad
swells, piano stabs, brass stabs, vocal chop loops, filtered intro, drop-stop-drop,

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish rap, female Spanish rap, rapid-fire delivery, drilling repetitive
phrasing, staccato cadence, chant hook, perreo chant hook, ad-lib heavy, spoken
interjections, whispered bars (sparingly), sing-rap hook, Auto-Tune lead,
double-tracked hook, stacked crowd shouts, DJ tags

Latin Trap
Latin Trap is Spanish-language trap from Puerto Rico’s late-2000s/2010s música
urbana scene. It fuses Southern U.S. trap with reggaeton-adjacent Caribbean swing,
delivered as gritty rap plus melodic, Auto-Tuned hooks in Spanish or Spanglish.

Genre Tags:
Latin trap, Trap latino, Trapetón, Urbano latino, Música urbana, Puerto Rico trap,
Reggaeton Trap, Latin hip hop, Spanish trap, Trap en español,

Mood Tags:
Menacing, dark, gritty, raw, brash, cocky, swaggering, streetwise, defiant,
aggressive, ruthless, tense, paranoid, icy, nocturnal, after-hours, hype, adrenaline,
urgent, relentless, toxic-flex, luxurious, decadent, seductive, provocative, moody,
melancholic, heartbroken, romantic, obsessive, confrontational, bitter, triumphant,
vengeful, unapologetic, claustrophobic, ominous, cold-focus, hungry, reckless,

Instrument Tags:
70–75 BPM half-time feel, 140–150 BPM double-time feel, 808 sub bass, distorted
808, 808 slides/glides, sub drops, punchy kick, clap/snare on 3, rimshot accents,
snare rolls, triplet hi-hats, 1/32 hat stutters, open-hat accents, perc ticks, sparse
cowbell, dark piano stabs, minor-key bell loop, detuned synth plucks, analog pad
haze, drone bass layer, brass stabs, reverse cymbals, risers, impact hits, siren FX,
vocal chops, filter sweeps, halftime breakdown, beat switch bridge,

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish rap, female Spanish rap, rapid-fire delivery, drilling repetitive
phrasing, staccato cadence, chant hook, perreo chant hook, ad-lib heavy, spoken
interjections, whispered bars (sparingly), sing-rap hook, Auto-Tune lead,
double-tracked hook, stacked crowd shouts, DJ tags

Regional Mexican
Regional Mexican is an umbrella for Mexican-rooted styles—banda, norteño,
mariachi, ranchera, and more. Shaped in Mexico and the U.S. Southwest. Largely
live-instrument driven, it favors strong grooves and storytelling lyrics.

Genre Tags:
Regional Mexican, Música mexicana, Música regional mexicana, Latin regional,
Banda, Banda sinaloense, Norteño, Norteño-banda, Sierreño, Sierreño-banda,
Corridos, Corridos verdes, Corridos tumbados, Narcocorridos, Ranchera, Mariachi,

Mood Tags:
Proud, soulful, nostalgic, sentimental, romantic, heartbroken, heartfelt, dramatic,
cinematic, triumphant, celebratory, festive, communal, rowdy, party, dance-ready,
gritty, streetwise, defiant, rebellious, tough, menacing, tense, intense, cocky, bold,
playful, flirtatious, patriotic, homesick, reflective, storytelling, raw, emotional,

Instrument Tags:
Accordion, diatonic button accordion, bajo sexto, bajo quinto, requinto guitar,
acoustic guitar strums, 12-string guitar, requinto lead runs, vihuela, guitarrón,
tololoche, electric bass, tuba bass, sousaphone, brass banda section, trumpets,
trombones, clarinets, alto horn, sax section, tambora, tarola snare, cymbal crashes,
hand percussion accents, polka/waltz groove, corrido gallop rhythm, mariachi violin

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, duet harmonies, tight thirds harmony, corrido
storytelling delivery, narrative verses, talk-sung lines, gritty rasp, clean bright tenor,
deep baritone, ranchera belting, mariachi-style projection, dramatic vibrato, “grito”
shouts, chanty hooks, ad-lib callouts, group shout accents, close-mic intimate vocal,
reverb-light vocal tone

Corridos
Corridos are traditional Mexican narrative ballads that report real events, heroes,
tragedies, and everyday life, often in four-line stanzas and commonly set to waltz or
polka rhythms. They’re now central to regional Mexican.

Genre Tags:
Corridos, Corrido, Corrido mexicano, Mexican corrido, Corrido tradicional, Corrido
norteño, Corrido fronterizo, Corrido revolucionario, Corrido político, Corrido social,

Mood Tags:
Storytelling, narrative, vivid, cinematic, epic, heroic, proud, defiant, rebellious, gritty,
raw, streetwise, tough, menacing, tense, intense, determined, hungry, fearless,
confrontational, triumphant, celebratory, communal, rowdy, nostalgic, homesick,
sentimental, bittersweet, heartbroken, romantic, earnest, heartfelt, dramatic,
mournful, reflective, warning-tale, moralizing, suspenseful, vengeful, cathartic

Instrument Tags:
Accordion lead, diatonic button accordion, bajo sexto rhythm, bajo quinto, acoustic
guitar strums, 12-string guitar shimmer, requinto lead runs, vihuela, guitarrón,
tololoche upright bass, electric bass, tuba bass line, brass hits, clarinet lines,
tambora bass drum, tarola snare, cymbal crashes, polka groove, fast waltz groove,
corrido gallop rhythm, instrumental intro, turnaround breaks,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, corrido storytelling delivery, narrative verses, talk-sung
lines, crisp diction, gritty rasp, clean bright tenor, deep baritone, dramatic vibrato,
duet harmonies, tight thirds harmony, grito shouts, spoken callouts, chanty hook,
Auto-Tune lead, ad-lib callouts, close-mic dry vocal tone, reverb-light vocals

tololoche bass, snare snaps, polka groove, instrumental intro, verse-heavy narrative, male

nothing but a name and a plan, final chorus push

Bachata
Bachata is Dominican guitar-led dance music in 4/4, born in working-class patios
and bars, blending bolero’s romantic song with Afro-Antillean rhythms. Bands lock
syncopated requinto/segunda patterns over bass, bongos, and güira; lyrics lean on
love, longing, and heartbreak.

Genre Tags:
Bachata, Dominican bachata, Traditional bachata, Bachata típica, Bachata moderna,
Modern bachata, Bachata urbana, Urban bachata, Bachata fusion, Bachata sensual,

Mood Tags:
Romantic, passionate, sensual, intimate, yearning, longing, wistful, nostalgic,
bittersweet, melancholic, heartbroken, tender, earnest, heartfelt, dreamy, warm,
soothing, affectionate, flirty, playful, cheeky, seductive, jealous, pleading,
confessional, late-night, after-hours, bittersweet-hopeful, sweet-sour, vulnerable,
dramatic, cathartic, smooth, swaying, danceable, hypnotic, glowing, sentimental

Instrument Tags:
Requinto lead guitar, segunda rhythm guitar, steel-string guitar, nylon-string guitar,
arpeggiated chord loops, syncopated guitar riffs, palm-muted guitar chanks, guitar
harmonics, chorus-guitar shimmer, reverb-washed guitar, bass guitar, sub-bass
layer, bongos, güira scrape, maracas, shaker, percussion fills, turnaround licks,
guitar fills, tambora, synth pads, keyboard layers, saxophone fills

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish vocals, female Spanish vocals, duet vocals, tight harmony thirds,
romantic croon, smooth tenor, deep baritone, smoky alto, breathy delivery,
passionate belting, melismatic runs, talk-sung lines, spoken ad-libs, stacked
harmonies, double-tracked chorus, intimate close-mic vocal, reverb-light vocals,

Salsa
Salsa is a New York–forged Afro-Caribbean dance music built on Cuban son/mambo
roots and Puerto Rican influence, organized around clave, piano montuno, and
bass tumbao, with congas, timbales, bongos/cowbell, and horn riffs. It developed in
NYC in the 1940s–50s, was labeled “salsa” in the 1960s, and peaked in the 1970s.

Genre Tags:
Salsa, NYC salsa, Nuyorican salsa, Salsa clásica, Salsa dura, Salsa brava, Salsa
romántica, Salsa de la mata, Afro-Cuban salsa, Salsa jazz,

Mood Tags:
Hot, fiery, ecstatic, celebratory, triumphant, infectious, high-energy, driving,
propulsive, urgent, intense, rowdy, party, communal, dancefloor, sweaty,
after-hours, playful, cheeky, flirty, romantic, passionate, sensual, bittersweet,
nostalgic, dramatic, proud, defiant, streetwise, gritty, bold, brassy, lush, elegant,
cinematic, euphoric, cathartic, rebellious, call-to-the-floor, shout-along,

Instrument Tags:
Clave (2-3 / 3-2), piano montuno, bass tumbao, conga tumbao/marcha, timbales
cascara, timbales mambo bell, bongo martillo, cowbell patterns, guiro, maracas,
claves, congas, timbales, bongos, horn section, trombone section, trumpet stabs,
sax, moña horn, mambo section hits, montuno section, breakdowns, stops and
punches, percussion-only breaks, descarga solos, charanga flute, charanga violins

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish vocals, female Spanish vocals, sonero lead, soneo improvisation,
shouted, grito shouts, rhythmic spoken lines, rapid-fire phrasing, melodically sung
chorus, romantic croon, dramatic vibrato, stacked coro harmonies, unison coro
chants, crowd shouts, “¡Azúcar!” shout, duet vocals, tight harmony thirds

stabs, moña horn lines, mambo section hits, coro-pregón, male Spanish vocals, sonero

Merengue
Merengue is the Dominican Republic’s emblematic fast dance music, typically built
on a driving 2/4 groove powered by tambora drum and güira scraper, with
syncopated “quintillo” figures.

Genre Tags:
Merengue, Dominican merengue, Merengue típico, Perico ripiao, Merengue
cibaeño, Merengue derecho, Pambiche, Merengue apambichao,

Mood Tags:
High-energy, explosive, festive, celebratory, euphoric, party, carnival, sweaty,
dancefloor, rowdy, playful, cheeky, humorous, flirtatious, brash, bold, triumphant,
confident, proud, communal, uplifting, urgent, relentless, kinetic, bouncy, bright,
sunny, fiery, electric, mischievous, sassy, provocative, streetwise, gritty, rebellious,
adrenaline, spiraling, unstoppable, happy-tears, playful-competitive

Instrument Tags:
Tambora, güira scraper, quintillo rhythm figure, diatonic button accordion,
accordion riffs, saxophone lead lines, paired saxophones, trumpet stabs, trombone
punches, horn section riffs, piano montuno-like vamps, electric bass, bass drops
(modern), congas, timbales fills, cowbell patterns, hi-hat drive, drum kit, synth
stabs, breakdown to tambora+güira, stop-time hits, riff-based mambo section,

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish vocals, female Spanish vocals, rapid syllabic delivery, chant chorus,
shouty callouts, playful double-entendre delivery, talk-sung lines, stacked chorus
vocals, unison hook chants, duet harmonies, hype ad-libs, crowd shouts, bright
nasal timbre (típico), smooth pop lead (orquesta), Auto-Tune lead (modern/urbano)

accordion lead riff, saxophone answers, electric bass drive, quick stop-time hits, breakdown
to tambora+güira, mambo section riffs, male Spanish vocals, rapid syllabic delivery, chant
chorus, song about turning payday into a street party, hard final hit

Mariachi
Mariachi is Mexican ensemble music rooted in western Mexico, featuring violins
and trumpets over vihuela, guitar, and guitarrón. Repertoire spans sones plus
rancheras, boleros, polkas, and waltzes. UNESCO inscribed mariachi on its
Intangible Cultural Heritage list in 2011

Genre Tags:
Mariachi, Mariachi tradicional, Mariachi moderno, Mariachi ranchera, Mariachi
huapango, Mariachi bolero, Mariachi son jalisciense, Mariachi son, Mariachi
jalisciense, Mariachi de serenata, Mariachi de plaza,

Mood Tags:
Proud, triumphant, celebratory, festive, communal, energetic, rowdy, bold, brassy,
dramatic, cinematic, heroic, defiant, heartfelt, sentimental, nostalgic, bittersweet,
romantic, passionate, yearning, sorrowful, cathartic, intense, spirited, playful,
flirtatious, elegant, stately, patriotic, homesick, reverent,

Instrument Tags:
Violins, trumpet section, vihuela mexicana rhythm, guitarrón bass lines,
nylon-string guitar, arpa jalisciense, violin tremolo, unison violin runs, trumpet
fanfare hits, trumpet doits/falls, rasgueado strums, son jalisciense groove, ranchera
strum pattern, huapango accents, bolero ballad feel, waltz pulse, polka drive,
stop-time punches, instrumental intro, violin/trumpet solos, ensemble hits,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, duet vocals, tight harmony thirds, mariachi-style
projection, ranchera belting, dramatic vibrato, emotional cry-break, grito shouts,
call-and-response coros, unison coro chants, spoken callouts (sparingly), close-mic
intimate verses, big chorus lift, ad-lib accents,

rasgueado rhythm, guitarrón bass pulse, stop-time punches, call-and-response coros, male
vocals, ranchera belting, dramatic vibrato, grito shouts, key change final chorus, song about
walking away from betrayal with dignity

Tango
Tango is an Afro-European dance born in Buenos Aires and Montevideo in the late
19th century. Built on phrased melodies over 2/4 or 4/4 and driven by the
bandoneón, it’s played by an orquesta típica.

Genre Tags:
Tango, Argentine tango, Uruguayan tango, Río de la Plata tango, Tango canción,
Tango instrumental, Orquesta típica, Guardia Vieja, Guardia Nueva,

Mood Tags:
Smoky, nocturnal, dramatic, cinematic, melancholic, bittersweet, nostalgic,
yearning, romantic, passionate, sensual, tense, dangerous, brooding, elegant,
refined, stately, proud, defiant, streetwise, gritty, hard-edged, suspenseful,
obsessive, jealous, mournful, intimate, confessional, hypnotic, pulsing, urgent,
sharp, cutting, haunted, cold-glamour, tragic, cathartic, stormy, restless

Instrument Tags:
Bandoneón lead, bandoneón tremolo, bandoneón stabs, bandoneón glissando,
violin section, unison violin runs, violin tremolo pads, piano tango accompaniment,
piano marcato, piano fills, double bass, bass walking accents, nylon guitar, flute,
orquesta típica ensemble hits, marcato in 4, syncopated accents, cortes, arrastre
pickups, yumba pulse, rubato intro, dramatic ritardando, staccato stops, moody
minor-key harmonies, counterpoint lines, percussion-less drive

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish vocals, female Spanish vocals, tango canción lead, deep baritone,
bright tenor, smoky alto, dramatic vibrato, declamatory delivery, story-sung verses,
conversational phrasing, rubato vocal timing, spoken intro, emotional cry-break,
tight close-mic vocal, roomy vintage reverb, callout ad-libs

Kizomba
Kizomba is an Angolan partner-dance music evolved from semba with Antillean
zouk influence. Slow-to-mid 4/4 groove with a heavy bassline and smooth melodic
hooks; often keyboard-led production.

Genre Tags:
Kizomba, Kizomba tradicional, Kizomba clássica, Kizomba roots, Kizomba moderna,
Kizomba romântica, Kizomba instrumental, Kizomba fusion, Afro-zouk,

Mood Tags:
Sensual, intimate, romantic, tender, warm, velvety, smooth, silky, sultry, late-night,
nocturnal, dim-lit, hypnotic, swaying, grounded, close, slow-burn, yearning, wistful,
bittersweet, melancholic, dreamy, soothing, relaxed, calm, understated, minimal,
deep, bass-heavy, glossy, elegant, flirtatious, seductive, confessional, vulnerable,
reflective, patient, affectionate, earnest, restrained, pulsing, steady, magnetic

Instrument Tags:
4/4 kick groove, deep sub-bass, strong electric bassline, bass guitar prominence,
rimshot/snare backbeat, syncopated percussion loop, conga/tumba hits, shakers,
hi-hat ticks, drum machine, keyboard-led production, warm synth pads, electric
piano/Rhodes chords, plucked synth hooks, clean electric guitar riffs, muted guitar
chanks, melodic lead guitar fills, saxophone, trumpet/sax accents, filtered intro
builds, breakdown-to-bass-and-percussion, stop-time drops, vocal chop accents

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, Portuguese vocals, Angolan Portuguese vocals, Cape
Verdean Creole vocals, French vocals, soft croon, breathy delivery, intimate
close-mic lead, smooth tenor, deep baritone, smoky alto, melodic hooks, talk-sung
lines, whispered ad-libs, stacked harmonies, Auto-Tune lead, reverb-light vocals

Bossa Nova
Bossa nova is a relaxed Brazilian style from late-1950s Rio, blending samba’s
syncopated 2/4 pulse with cool-jazz harmony. Centered on nylon-string guitar
comping, piano/bass, light drums/percussion, and intimate vocals;

Genre Tags:
Bossa nova, Brazilian bossa nova, Rio bossa nova, Samba-jazz, Brazilian jazz,
Cool-jazz samba fusion, Bossa nova instrumental, Bossa nova vocal,

Mood Tags:
Cool, laid-back, breezy, smooth, mellow, intimate, understated, subtle, soft, tender,
romantic, affectionate, nostalgic, bittersweet, melancholic, dreamy, warm, relaxed,
elegant, refined, classy, soothing, calm, gentle, swaying, cozy, content, hopeful,
contemplative, sentimental, quiet-confidence, airy, light, flirtatious, delicate,

Instrument Tags:
Nylon-string guitar, fingerstyle guitar comping, syncopated guitar groove, thumbed
bass notes on guitar, jazz chord voicings, extended chords, piano comping, piano
montuno-like vamp, upright bass, electric bass, brushed drum kit, cross-stick snare,
hi-hat ticks, ride cymbal soft, shaker, agogô bells, pandeiro, soft congas, clave-like
stick pattern, gentle percussion fills, chromatic turnarounds, ii–V–I progressions,
lush reharmonization, melodic guitar fills, saxophone melody, flute melody

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, Portuguese vocals, English vocals, intimate close-mic
vocal, soft croon, breathy delivery, understated delivery, mellow tenor, deep
baritone, smoky alto, light vibrato, minimal melisma, conversational phrasing, quiet
phrasing behind the beat, double-tracked chorus, stacked harmonies, whispered
ad-libs

Latin Rock
Latin Rock fuses rock’s guitars and drum kit with Afro-Latin/Caribbean rhythms and
hand percussion (congas, timbales), often with Spanish/Portuguese or bilingual
vocals. It emerged in U.S. Latino scenes and broke through with Santana in the late
1960s, later feeding Latin alternative and rock en español crossovers.

Genre Tags:
Latin rock, Chicano rock, East L.A. rock, Latin psychedelic rock, Latin blues rock,
Latin funk rock, Latin jazz-rock, Afro-Latin rock fusion, Salsa-rock fusion

Mood Tags:
Fiery, sweaty, celebratory, high-energy, dancefloor, percussive, groove-heavy,
urgent, rebellious, defiant, streetwise, gritty, bold, brash, triumphant, party,
communal, anthemic, soulful, funky, trippy, raw, sexy, flirtatious, romantic,
nostalgic, festival, ecstatic, cathartic, wild, chaotic, upbeat, intense, spirited, fearless

Instrument Tags:
Overdriven electric guitar, wah-wah lead guitar, bluesy guitar bends, guitar
feedback, funk guitar, palm-muted riffs, drum kit backbeat, syncopated kick, tom
fills, percussion breaks, congas, timbales cascara, timbales mambo bell, bongos,
cowbell, güiro, claves, maracas, shakers, Latin percussion, electric bass, tumbao
bass feel, Hammond organ, electric piano, piano montuno, horn section riffs,
trumpet, trombone, sax solos, extended jam solos, stop-time punches

Vocal Tags:
Male Spanish vocals, female Spanish vocals, bilingual vocals, English vocals, gritty
baritone, bright tenor, soulful lead singing, bluesy rasp, chant hook, gang vocals,
coro backing vocals, unison shouts, spoken callouts, ad-lib hype shouts, melodic
chorus lift, harmony thirds, double-tracked chorus, live-room vocal feel,

Baile Funk
Baile funk is Rio de Janeiro club music from favela parties shaped in the 1980s–90s
from Miami bass/electro. It runs on hard 4/4 kick, tamborzão percussion, booming
bass, and call-and-response MC vocals in Brazilian Portuguese.

Genre Tags:
Baile funk, Funk carioca, Brazilian funk, Favela funk, Funk de baile, Batidão,
Tamborzão, Volt-mix, Proibidão, Funk melody, Funk ostentação,

Mood Tags:
Explosive, raw, gritty, aggressive, confrontational, chaotic, hyped, high-energy,
relentless, sweaty, party, celebratory, euphoric, brash, bold, swaggering, streetwise,
rebellious, defiant, provocative, raunchy, explicit, playful, cheeky, flirtatious,
percussive, bass-crazed, tense, dangerous, menacing, edgy, noisy, lo-fi, distorted,
rebellious-joy, call-to-the-floor, chant-ready, rowdy, adrenaline,

Instrument Tags:
Hard 4/4 kick, sub-bass, 808 subs, distorted 808, tamborzão loop, kick/snare
tamborzão pattern, rimshot snaps, clap layers, offbeat cymbals, open-hat bursts,
hat rolls, snare rushes, atabaque drum samples, percussion one-shots, sirens,
airhorns, DJ drops, crowd noise, vocal chop stabs, gritty sample hits, minimal synth
stabs, noise sweeps, risers, hit-stop drops, pitch-bent FX, hard limiter loudness

Vocal Tags:
Male Portuguese vocals, female Portuguese vocals, MC callouts, chant hook,
call-and-response chants, gang shouts, hype ad-libs, fast talk-rap, staccato flow,
shout-rap, melodic sing-rap hooks, monotone taunt delivery, spoken threats
(proibidão), explicit lyrical content, crowd-response vocals, double-tracked hook,
distorted vocal FX, vocal chops

Brasil Funk
Brasil Funk (Brazilian funk) is baile-party music: hard 4/4 kick, booming bass,
tamborzão percussion, and Portuguese MC. Emerging in the 1980s from Miami
bass/electro, it spread nationwide.

Genre Tags:
Brasil funk, Brazilian funk, Funk carioca, Baile funk, Funk de baile, Batidão,
Tamborzão, Volt-mix, Proibidão, Funk melody, Funk ostentação, Funk paulista

Mood Tags:
Explosive, raw, gritty, hyped, high-energy, relentless, sweaty, percussive,
bass-crazed, brash, bold, streetwise, rebellious, defiant, confrontational, chaotic,
rowdy, chant-ready, provocative, playful, flirty, raunchy, edgy, menacing, tense,
dangerous, aggressive, noisy, lo-fi, distorted, adrenaline, euphoric, party, carnival,
triumphant, cocky, frantic, infectious, hard-hitting, unapologetic

Instrument Tags:
Hard 4/4 kick, tamborzão groove, kick-snare tamborzão pattern, blown-out
sub-bass, 808 subs, distorted 808 slides, rimshot snaps, clap layers, offbeat hats,
open-hat bursts, rapid hat rolls, snare rushes, percussion, atabaque/surdo-style
drum samples, metallic perc hits, cowbell leads, sirens, airhorns, DJ drops, crowd
noise, hit-stop drops, breakbeat cuts, risers, noise sweeps, synth stabs, brass synth
stabs, pitch-bent FX

Vocal Tags:
Male Portuguese vocals, female Portuguese vocals, shout-rap, fast talk-rap, staccato
flow, chant hook, chants, gang shouts, hype ad-libs, taunting delivery, monotone
chant delivery, melodic sing-rap hook, explicit lyrical content, crowd-response
vocals, double-tracked hook, distorted vocal FX

Brazilian Phonk
Brazilian Phonk is an internet-borne hybrid where Brazilian funk percussion meets
drift-phonk’s cowbell-led, distorted, house-like drops. Huge clipped low-end,
sirens/whistles, rapid breakdowns, and Portuguese MC callouts.

Genre Tags:
Brazilian Phonk, Brazilian phonk, Phonk brasileiro, Funk automotivo, Automotivo,
Montagem, Montagem phonk, Funk mandelão, Mandelao, Funk bruxaria, Bruxaria,

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, blown-out, brutal, relentless, hyped, adrenaline, high-intensity, feral,
chaotic, abrasive, distorted, overdriven, bass-crazed, menacing, dark, eerie, sinister,
hostile, confrontational, gritty, raw, noisy, reckless, urgent, frantic, turbo,
high-speed, racing, night-drive, maximal, overstimulated, rowdy, chant-ready,
command-heavy, rebellious, streetwise, dangerous, edgy, industrial, mechanical,
club-rage, workout, power, swaggering, unhinged, punchy, volatile, trashy-fun

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, hard clipped kick, tamborzão, funk mandelão swing, funk
bruxaria “tuin” screech, wall-of-sub low end, car-trunk speaker bass, distorted 808
subs, 808 cowbell melody, rave supersaw stabs, siren risers, airhorn hits, assovio
whistle, metallic perc hits, rimshot snaps, clap stacks, open-hat, rapid hat rolls,
snare rushes, drops, pitch-bent FX, hard limiting, saturation, DJ tags, vocal chop

Vocal Tags:
Male Brazilian Portuguese MC, female Brazilian Portuguese MC, hoarse command
shouts, chants, ad-libs, short hook chants, shout-rap delivery, taunting monotone,
fast clipped phrasing, distorted vocal FX, chopped Memphis rap, pitched-down
vocal chops, sped-up vocal chops, barely-audible vox under distortion

Afrobeats
Afrobeats is contemporary West African pop blending local rhythms with dancehall,
hip hop, R&B, soca and more. It’s driven by syncopated percussion and deep bass
with catchy melodic hooks.

Genre Tags:
Afrobeats, Afropop, West African pop, Naija Afrobeats, Ghanaian Afrobeats,
Afro-fusion, Afro-pop crossover, Afro dancehall, Afro R&B, Afro-rap, Afrobeats club,

Mood Tags:
Feel-good, sunny, upbeat, danceable, infectious, bouncy, vibrant, celebratory, party,
flirtatious, romantic, sensual, warm, confident, cool, smooth, silky, glossy, playful,
cheeky, carefree, communal, triumphant, bold, uplifting, hopeful, breezy,
summer-night, late-night, hypnotic, pulsing, groovy, soulful, nostalgic, bittersweet,
tender, yearning, sultry, mellow, reflective, dreamy,

Instrument Tags:
Syncopated drum patterns, drum machine grooves, punchy kick, snappy
snare/clap, rimshot accents, offbeat hats, shaker layers, conga loops,
talking-drum-style hits, 808 sub-bass, rolling basslines, bass guitar pocket, plucked
synth lead, marimba-like plucks, bright synth stabs, warm pads, detuned keys,
clean electric guitar riffs, palm-muted guitar chops, piano/Rhodes chords, horn
stabs, percussion breaks, stop-time drops, filtered intro builds, FX risers

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, Nigerian Pidgin vocals, English vocals, Yoruba, Igbo, Twi,
melodic sing-rap, smooth croon, bright tenor, deep baritone, smoky alto, chant
hook, stacked harmonies, double-tracked hook, ad-lib heavy, hype callouts, rap
verse feature, talk-sung phrasing, intimate close-mic vocal

Afro House
Afro House is a South African house subgenre blending 4/4 club grooves,
deep/progressive chords, and polyrhythmic African percussion. Often 100–123 BPM

Genre Tags:
Afro house, Afro-house, Afrohouse,Afro deep house, Tribal house,

Mood Tags:
Hypnotic, percussive, tribal, earthy, spiritual, uplifting, euphoric, warm, sunset,
ceremonial, grounded, deep, moody, soulful, yearning, romantic, sensual, intimate,
meditative, expansive, cinematic, triumphant, confident, joyous, communal,
ecstatic, relentless, pulsing, mysterious, lush, airy, dusty, raw, organic, glossy,
dreamy, cathartic, blissful, stormy-build, tension-release, ritualistic, transportive

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, deep sub-bass, rolling bassline, syncopated percussion
loops, congas, bongos, djembé hits, talking-drum-style fills, shakers, cabasa, claves,
rimshot snaps, open-hat lifts, tom runs, handclap stacks, polyrhythmic drum layers,

Afro Fusion
Afro Fusion (Afrofusion) is genre-blending Afrobeats/Afropop that keeps West
African rhythmic DNA but folds in dancehall, hip hop, R&B, reggae, and pop.

Genre Tags:
Afro Fusion, Afrofusion, Afro-fusion, Afrobeats fusion, Afropop fusion, Afro-pop
crossover, Afro-fusion pop, Afro-fusion R&B, Afro-fusion dancehall,

Mood Tags:
Feel-good, warm, sunny, vibrant, bouncy, groovy, infectious, danceable, celebratory,
confident, flirtatious, romantic, sensual, smooth, glossy, breezy, carefree, playful,
cheeky, triumphant, uplifting, hopeful, resilient, streetwise, bold, gritty, soulful,
yearning, bittersweet, nostalgic, introspective, conscious, defiant, communal,
late-night, hypnotic, pulsing

Instrument Tags:
Syncopated Afrobeat/Afrobeats drum patterns, drum machine groove, punchy kick,
snappy snare/clap, rimshot accents, offbeat hats, shaker layers, conga/percussion
loops, talking-drum-style fills, dembow flavor, trap hat rolls, 808 sub-bass, rolling
basslines, bass guitar pocket, highlife guitar riffs, palm-muted guitar chops, reggae
skank guitar (optional), Rhodes/piano chords, warm synth pads, plucked synth
hooks, marimba/kalimba plucks, bright synth stabs, horn stabs, vocal chop hooks,
filter builds, breakdown to drums+bass, beat-switch bridge, FX risers/delay throws

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, Nigerian Pidgin vocals, English vocals, Yoruba phrases,
Igbo phrases, Twi phrases, melodic sing-rap, smooth croon, dancehall toasting, rap
verse feature, chant hook, stacked harmonies, double-tracked hook, ad-lib heavy,
hype callouts, intimate close-mic lead, light Auto-Tune lead

Amapiano
Amapiano is a South African house offshoot, defined by jazzy piano riffs, lush
chords, shuffling percussion, and the signature log drum bassline. 108–115 BPM

Genre Tags:
Amapiano, South African house, Piano house, Amapiano groove, Amapiano
instrumental, Amapiano vocal, Amapiano club, Amapiano street mix,

Mood Tags:
Hypnotic, groovy, bouncy, laid-back, smooth, warm, soulful, uplifting, celebratory,
feel-good, late-night, afterhours, seductive, romantic, intimate, confident, cool,
glossy, silky, dreamy, euphoric, cathartic, communal, playful, flirtatious,
relaxed-energy, steady, rolling, streetwise, triumphant, nostalgic, moody, deep,
meditative, spiritual, airy, lush, propulsive, addictive, simmering, tension-release

Instrument Tags:
Log drum bassline, log-drum rolls, sub-bass pulses, rolling bassline, shuffled drum
groove, syncopated kick, snare/clap snaps, rimshot accents, open-hat lifts, shaker
layers, cabasa, conga loops, bongos, tom fills, percussion one-shots, piano riffs, jazz
chord voicings, Rhodes/electric piano, warm synth pads, string-synth swells,
marimba/kalimba plucks, airy pluck leads, vocal chop hooks, filter builds,
breakdown to drums+log drum, drop with silence hits, FX risers, delay throws

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, South African-language vocals, IsiZulu vocals, IsiXhosa
vocals, Sesotho vocals, Setswana vocals, English vocals, chant hook, group chants,
hype callouts, melodic topline, sing-rap phrasing, talk-sung lines, ad-libs, tight
close-mic lead, stacked harmonies, ululation accents

Dancehall
Dancehall is Jamaican popular music from Kingston dancehalls, built on “riddims”
where a deejay sings over bass-and-drum tracks.

Genre Tags:
Dancehall, Jamaican dancehall, Dancehall reggae, Ragga, Raggamuffin,

Mood Tags:
Rowdy, high-energy, hyped, brash, aggressive, confrontational, gritty, streetwise,
cocky, defiant, rebellious, chaotic, wild, sweaty, dancefloor, party, celebratory,
euphoric, infectious, playful, cheeky, raunchy, provocative, flirtatious, sensual,
seductive, confident, triumphant, anthemic, chant-ready, menacing, tense,
dangerous, dark, cold, moody, nighttime, bass-heavy, percussive, punchy, bouncy,

Instrument Tags:
Riddim-driven beat, digital riddim, Sleng Teng-style digital groove, dembow drum
pattern, punchy kick, snappy snare, rimshot snaps, clap layers, offbeat hats,
open-hat bursts, hat rolls, syncopated percussion, shaker layers, tambourine ticks,
skank guitar chops, bubble keyboard/organ, synth stabs, horn stabs, deep
sub-bass, 808 bass drops, bassline wobble, dub delay throws, spring reverb hits,
tape echo FX, sirens, airhorns, rewind/selector drops, stop-time breaks, dropout
drops, crowd noise

Vocal Tags:
Male Jamaican Patois vocals, female Jamaican Patois vocals, deejay toasting, singjay
vocals, talk-rap cadence, chant hook, soundclash callouts, hype ad-libs, gunfinger
shouts, melodic chorus lift, half-sung hook, rapid-fire delivery, staccato phrasing,
gritty rasp, confident baritone, bright tenor, dancehall sing-song flow,
double-tracked hook, stacked harmonies, megaphone vocal FX

snare, offbeat hats, shaker layers, deep sub-bass, skank guitar chops, bubble organ, synth
stabs, siren FX, stop-time breaks, male Jamaican Patois vocals, deejay toasting, chant hook,

Reggae
Reggae is Jamaican popular music that emerged in the late 1960s from
ska/rocksteady, defined by a slower groove, heavy bass-and-drums, and the offbeat
“skank” accent (often emphasizing beats 2 and 4). It grew through sound-system
culture and often carries social commentary, spirituality, and love/party themes.

Genre Tags:
Reggae, Jamaican reggae, Roots reggae, Classic reggae

Mood Tags:
Laid-back, warm, breezy, relaxed, mellow, soulful, heartfelt, romantic, tender,
bittersweet, nostalgic, hopeful, uplifting, positive, joyful, celebratory, party,
easygoing, confident, cool, smooth, groove-heavy, earthy, grounded, spiritual,
meditative, reverent, conscious, defiant, rebellious, righteous, militant, resolute,
gritty, smoky, nighttime, intimate, communal, serene, soothing, comforting,

Instrument Tags:
Offbeat guitar, one-drop drum beat, steppers kick pattern, rimshot/snare
cross-stick, hi-hat ticks, shaker, tambourine, hand percussion, deep electric
bassline, bassline, bubble organ, piano chops, horn riffs, trombone stabs, trumpet
lines, sax fills, melodica lead, Nyabinghi hand drums, spring reverb hits, tape
echo/dub delay throws, dropouts, dub siren FX, live-room drums

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, Jamaican vocals, English vocals, conscious lyric delivery,
protest themes, spiritual themes, love-song themes, singjay, toasting, smooth
croon, gritty rasp, bright tenor, warm baritone, chant hook, stacked harmonies,
group choruses, reverb-washed hooks, dub-style vocal drops, ad-lib callouts

rimshot cross-stick, hi-hat ticks, bubble organ, horn section riffs, tape echo throws,
verse-chorus-bridge, male vocals, Jamaican Patois, conscious delivery, song about staying
hopeful while the world feels heavy, fade outro

Calypso
Calypso (aka kaiso) is Trinidad and Tobago’s Carnival song tradition, where
calypsonians deliver witty, satirical, topical storytelling over syncopated dance
grooves. Anchored by strong bass, offbeat rhythm guitar/keys, percussion, and
horns/steelpan—serving as party music, social “news,” and political commentary.

Genre Tags:
Calypso, Kaiso, Trinidad & Tobago calypso, Carnival calypso, Calypso tent,

Mood Tags:
Festive, Carnival, celebratory, joyful, sunny, breezy, danceable, bouncy, upbeat,
communal, singalong, call-and-response, party, playful, cheeky, humorous, witty,
ironic, satirical, teasing, competitive, boastful, flirtatious, suggestive, mischievous,
storytelling, narrative, topical, observant, sharp, rebellious, defiant, political,
protest, righteous, socially conscious, bold, streetwise, rowdy, spirited, proud,

Instrument Tags:
Syncopated groove, walking basslines, electric/upright bass, drum kit, snare
backbeat, rimshot patterns, congas, bongos, shakers, maracas, cowbell, claves,
hand percussion, steelpan/steel drums, brass section, trumpet, trombone,
saxophone, clarinet, rhythm guitar chops, cuatro, acoustic guitar, piano comping,
organ/bubble keyboard, melodic fills, horn riffs, band hits, live-room band sound

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, calypsonian lead, Trinidadian English Creole delivery,
storytelling verses, witty punchline phrasing, satirical commentary, double
entendre, spoken-sung cadence, sing-song hooks, call-and-response chorus, crowd
participation, chant-style refrains, comedic timing, competitive “picong” taunts,
topical/political lyric focus, group chorus shouts, announcer-style callouts

Flamenco
Flamenco is an Andalusian Spanish art form uniting cante, toque, baile and palmas,
organized by compás and palos like soleá and bulerías. Strongly linked to Gitano
culture, it was inscribed by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2010.

Genre Tags:
Flamenco, Andalusian flamenco, Gitano flamenco, Cante, Toque, Baile, Palmas,
Zapateado, Compás, Duende, Tablao flamenco, Juerga flamenca, Cante jondo,

Mood Tags:
Passionate, fiery, intense, raw, cathartic, dramatic, proud, defiant, aching, mournful,
tragic, haunted, desperate, yearning, sensual, seductive, jealous, confrontational,
bold, electric, frenetic, playful, teasing, witty, flirtatious, ecstatic, ritualistic, spiritual,
reverent, intimate, vulnerable, gritty, urgent, volatile, suspenseful, spellbinding,
soul-baring, virtuosic, explosive, simmering, relentless, pulse-driven

Instrument Tags:
Flamenco guitar, Rasgueado strums, Picado runs, Alzapúa, Tremolo, Golpe,
Golpeador hits, Cajón, Palmas claras, Palmas sordas, Finger snaps, Foot stomps,
Zapateado percussion, Castanets, Jaleo shouts, Hand percussion, Shakers,
Tambourine accents, Hand drum accents, Live-room handclap, Nylon-string
brightness, Phrygian-mode riffs, Drone pedal tones, Call-and-response guitar
falsetas, Percussion breaks, Violin, Flute, Bass guitar

Vocal Tags:
Male cantaor, Female cantaora, Cante jondo, Cante chico delivery, Quejío,
Melismatic runs, Microtonal bends, Gravelly rasp, Bright piercing tone, Deep chest
voice, Rubato entrances, Compás-locked phrasing, Dramatic swells, Shouted
accents, Long held notes, Rapid-fire lyrics, Percussive consonants, Group responses

Arabic Pop
Arabic Pop (Arab pop) is mainstream Arabic-language pop that merges Western
pop hooks/production with Arabic melodic phrasing and regional rhythms.

Genre Tags:
Arabic Pop, Arab Pop, Middle Eastern Pop, Pan-Arab Pop, Arabic Dance-Pop,

Mood Tags:
Romantic, yearning, bittersweet, heartbroken, devotional, tender, warm, breezy,
sunny, euphoric, celebratory, party, dancefloor, flirtatious, sensual, seductive,
confident, swaggering, playful, cheeky, dramatic, passionate, longing, nostalgic,
hopeful, empowering, triumphant, sassy, defiant, jealous, resentful, apologetic,
vulnerable, intimate, late-night, glossy, high-energy, bouncy, anthemic, singalong,

Instrument Tags:
Darbuka/doumbek, riq, frame drum/daff, handclaps, shaker layers, maqsum
groove, malfuf groove, saidi rhythm, khaliji percussion, percussion fills, drum
machine, crisp kick/snare, trap hat rolls (optional), dembow accents (optional),
synth bass, 808 sub, bass guitar groove, string orchestra pads, Arabic violin solo,
qanun glissandos, oud riffs, nay flute lines, accordion, electric guitar hooks, acoustic
guitar strums, keyboard arpeggios, synth plucks, synth brass stabs, orchestral hits,
risers, vocal chop hooks, delay throws, reverb swells,

Vocal Tags:
Male Arabic vocals, female Arabic vocals, Egyptian Arabic vocals, Levantine Arabic
vocals, Gulf Arabic vocals, English hook crossover, melismatic runs, tarab
ornamentation, mawwal intro, chant hook, big belted chorus, breathy close-mic
verses, airy head voice, gritty rasp, light Auto-Tune lead, stacked harmonies,
double-tracked hook, ad-lib heavy, spoken callouts

Bollywood
Bollywood music is Hindi film songs. Hooky vocals and dance numbers from
Mumbai’s Hindi-language cinema. It blends raga-flavored melodic phrasing, folk
rhythms, and orchestral/synth pop production.

Genre Tags:
Bollywood, Bollywood music, Hindi film music, Hindi film songs, Filmi, Filmi geet,
Bollywood soundtrack, Bollywood dance-pop, Bollywood romantic ballad,

Mood Tags:
Romantic, yearning, bittersweet, heartbroken, devotional, tender, warm, breezy,
sunny, euphoric, celebratory, party, dancefloor, flirtatious, sensual, seductive,
confident, swaggering, playful, cheeky, dramatic, passionate, longing, nostalgic,
hopeful, empowering, triumphant, sassy, defiant, jealous, resentful, apologetic,
vulnerable, intimate, late-night, glossy, high-energy, bouncy, anthemic, singalong,

Instrument Tags:
Darbuka/doumbek, riq, frame drum/daff, handclaps, shaker layers, maqsum
groove, malfuf groove, saidi rhythm, khaliji percussion, percussion fills, drum
machine, crisp kick/snare, dembow accents, synth bass, 808 sub, bass guitar
groove, string orchestra pads, Arabic violin solo, qanun glissandos, oud riffs, nay
flute lines, accordion, electric guitar hooks, acoustic guitar strums, keyboard
arpeggios, synth plucks, synth brass stabs, orchestral hits, risers,

Vocal Tags:
Male Arabic vocals, female Arabic vocals, Egyptian Arabic vocals, Levantine Arabic
vocals, Gulf Arabic vocals, melismatic runs, tarab ornamentation, mawwal intro,
chant hook, gritty rasp, stacked harmonies, double-tracked hook

Classical
Classical (Western classical) is the written art-music tradition of Europe and its
descendants. It favors notation, formal development, and acoustic ensembles. Key
figures: J.S. Bach, Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven, Schubert, Chopin, Wagner

Genre Tags:
Classical music, Western classical, Art music, Orchestral, Symphonic, Chamber
music, String quartet, Piano sonata, Sonata form, Symphony, Concerto, Overture,

Mood Tags:
Majestic, Noble, Elegant, Refined, Poised, Bright, Buoyant, Triumphant, Heroic,
Solemn, Reverent, Sacred, Devotional, Awe, Grand, Ceremonial, Stately, Regal,
Dramatic, Tense, Suspenseful, Turbulent, Stormy, Urgent, Restless, Melancholic,
Tragic, Lamenting, Tender, Romantic, Yearning, Sentimental, Introspective, Serene,
Pastoral, Warm, Comforting, Mystical, Uncanny, Ominous, Playful, Whimsical,

Instrument Tags:
Full orchestra, String section, First violin lead, Viola pads, Cello melody, Double
bass, Pizzicato strings, Tremolo strings, Harp glissando, Solo piano, Concert grand
piano, Fortepiano, Harpsichord, Pipe organ, Solo violin, Solo cello, String quartet,
Woodwinds choir, Flute runs, Oboe lyrical lead, Clarinet warm lead, Bassoon
counterlines, French horns, Trumpet fanfare, Trombone weight, Tuba pedal tones,
Timpani rolls, Snare drum (march), Cymbal crashes, Triangle sparkle

Vocal Tags:
Operatic soprano, Operatic mezzo-soprano, Operatic tenor, Operatic baritone,
Operatic bass, Bel canto delivery, Vibrato-rich singing, Dramatic aria, Lyric aria,
Recitative passages, Choral SATB, Chamber choir, Instrumental

Contemporary Classical
Contemporary Classical is Western art music written in the late-20th/21st centuries
(post-1945). It often uses notation, extended techniques, and electronics. Key
figures include Pierre Boulez, John Cage, Steve Reich, Philip Glass, Arvo Pärt, György

Genre Tags:
Contemporary classical, Modern classical, New music, Post-1945 classical,

Baroque
Baroque is Western art music c.1600–1750, marked by basso continuo, strong tonal
harmony, dramatic contrasts, ornate melody, and dense counterpoint. Key figures:
Monteverdi, Corelli, Vivaldi, Handel, J.S. Bach, Lully, Purcell.

Genre Tags:
Baroque, Baroque music, Early Baroque, Middle Baroque, Late Baroque, High
Baroque, Baroque orchestra, Baroque chamber music, Baroque sacred music,

Mood Tags:
Dramatic, Theatrical, Grand, Regal, Stately, Ceremonial, Majestic, Noble,
Triumphant, Exultant, Exuberant, Energetic, Driving, Lively, Spirited, Playful, Witty,
Ornate, Opulent, Florid, Intricate, Intellectual, Suspenseful, Tense, Restless,
Turbulent, Dark, Ominous, Solemn, Reverent, Devotional, Sacred, Melancholic,
Tender, Intimate, Pastoral, Radiant, Glorious, Awe-struck, Yearning, Resolute

Instrument Tags:
Basso continuo, Harpsichord continuo, Pipe organ continuo, Figured bass, Theorbo,
Lute, Baroque guitar, Viola da gamba, Violone, Baroque cello, Baroque violin, Violin
section ripieno, Recorder consort, Baroque flute, Baroque oboe, Bassoon, Natural
trumpet, Natural horn, Cornett, Sackbut, Timpani, String tremolo, Pizzicato bass,
Sequence patterns, Circle-of-fifths motion, Pedal point, Ornamental trills, Mordents

Vocal Tags:

Homophonic chorus blocks, Latin liturgical text, Italian opera diction
